Official name: William M. (Mac) Thornberry National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2021, Division A, Title XCII, Section 9203 ("Spectrum information technology modernization efforts")
Casual name: FY2021 NDAA, Division A, Title XCII, Section 9203 ("Spectrum information technology modernization efforts")
Short summary: A directive for covered agencies, including the Department of Defense, to employ updated algorithms, data, and AI tools in modernizing their infrastructure for managing Federal spectrum. This involves enhancing efficiency, validation, accuracy, modeling, and monitoring, with a specific emphasis on automated adjustments, collaborative spectrum sharing, and interoperability, while also mandating comprehensive plans, reviews, and oversight to ensure effective implementation and integration of these AI-driven advancements.
Long summary:
Use updated algorithms, data, AI tools, and algorithms to modernize infrastructure. Ways to improve infrastructure include efficiency, validation, accuracy, modeling, and monitoring. The directive aims to enhance covered agencies' capacity to meet mission requirements in congested Federal spectrum environments through the implementation of automated adjustments, the creation of a time-based mechanism for collaborative spectrum sharing among agencies, and fostering collaboration to ensure interoperability. 

Within 240 days of the enactment of this Act, the Assistant Secretary of Commerce for Communications and Information is required to submit to Congress a report outlining a plan for the National Telecommunications and Information Administration (NTIA) to modernize and automate its infrastructure for managing the use of Federal spectrum by covered agencies, with the goal of improving efficiency in spectrum management in ways such as a timeline for implementation, plans for security enhancement, data model improvement, and workflow automation. 

Within one year of the enactment of this Act, the head of each covered agency is required to submit a report to the Assistant Secretary of Commerce for Communications and Information and the Policy and Plans Steering Group outlining a plan to modernize the agency's infrastructure. Each submitted report must include an assessment of their current capabilities, a timeline for completion, management capabilities, agency requirements or restrictions, existing infrastructure, and a plan for safe integration of government data.

The Comptroller General of the United States is mandated to conduct a review of covered agencies' infrastructure within 180 days of the Act's enactment and subsequently oversee the implementation of modernization plans submitted by the Assistant Secretary and covered agencies, providing regular reports and briefings to relevant congressional committees until December 31, 2030. The term "covered agency" includes any Federal entity, as determined by the Assistant Secretary, encompassing the Department of Defense, and "infrastructure" is defined as information technology systems and related tools and databases.

Official name: William M. (Mac) Thornberry National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2021, Division A, Title XC, Section 9004 ("Department of Homeland Security reports on digital content forgery technology")
Casual name: FY2021 NDAA, Division A, Title XC, Section 9004 ("Department of Homeland Security reports on digital content forgery technology")
Short summary: Instructs the Secretary of the Department of Homeland Security to submit annually, for a period of 5 years, a report to Congress on the state of digital content forgery technology.
Long summary:
Instructs the Secretary of the Department of Homeland Security to submit annually, for a period of 5 years, a report to Congress on the state of digital content forgery technology. 

Requires the report to include an assessment of the underlying technologies used to create or propagate digital content forgeries, including the evolution of such technologies and patterns of dissemination of such technologies. 

Requires the report to include a description of the types of digital content forgeries, including those used to commit fraud, cause harm, harass, coerce, or silence vulnerable groups or individuals, or violate civil rights.

Requires the report to include an assessment of how foreign governments, proxies and networks thereof, and non-governmental entities use, or could use, digital content forgeries to harm national security. 

Requires the report to include an assessment of the uses, applications, dangers, benefits, and impact on individuals of deep learning or digital content forgery technologies used to generate realistic depictions of events that did not occur. 

Requires the report to include a description of the methods and technological countermeasures that are, or could be, used to identify forgeries and address concerns with digital content forgery technology.

Long summary:

Official name: National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2022, Section 216 ("Improvements relating to steering committee on emerging technology and national security threats")
Casual name: FY2022 NDAA, Section 216 ("Improvements relating to steering committee on emerging technology and national security threats")
Short summary: Amends Section 236 of the FY 2021 National Defense Authorization Act by changing the structure of a steering committee on emerging technology and national security and featuring the intelligence community more prominently in the steering committee's responsibilities.
Long summary:
Amends Section 236 of the FY 2021 National Defense Authorization Act by adding the Director of National Intelligence as an official that can, with the Secretary of Defense, establish a steering committee on emerging technology and national security and receive recommendations from the committee. Amends members of the steering committee to consist of the Principal Deputy Director of National Intelligence, the Deputy Sector of Defense, the Vice Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staffs, and other appropriate officials. Adds that the Steering Committee shall be chaired by the Deputy Secretary of Defense, the Vice Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, and the Principal Deputy Director of National Intelligence jointly.

Amends the responsibilities of the steering committee by clarifying that the committee's strategy is to maintain the technological superiority of both the US military and intelligence community according to not only the National Defense Strategy, but also the National Intelligence Strategy and the National Security Strategy. Adds that the steering committee is responsible for making recommendations on any changes to the guidance for developing the National Intelligence Program budget and determining whether sufficient resources are available for the intelligence community, in addition to the Department of Defense, to defeat threats to the US.

Adds that the list of technologies classified as emerging technologies is to be determined jointly by the Secretary of Defense and the Director of National Intelligence. Adds the definition of intelligence community from the National Security Act of 1947.

Long summary:
Mandates compatibility of information technology or software systems with new and emerging technologies, such as artificial intelligence.

Official name: James M. Inhofe National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2023, Section 1554 ("Roadmap and implementation plan for cyber adoption of artificial intelligence")
Casual name: FY2023 NDAA, Section 1554 ("Roadmap and implementation plan for cyber adoption of artificial intelligence")
Short summary: Requires the Commander of the United States Cyber Command and the Chief Information Officer of the Department of Defense to develop a five-year implementation plan for rapidly adopting and acquiring artificial intelligence systems to support the Department of Defense.
Long summary:
Requires the Commander of the United States Cyber Command and the Chief Information Officer of the Department of Defense to develop a five-year implementation plan for rapidly adopting artificial intelligence systems to support the Department of Defense. 

The plan shall: 

(a) Identify and prioritize AI systems and data that currently affect, or may benefit in the future, the Department's cybersecurity and cyber operations. 

(b) Lay out a plan to acquire AI systems and data identified in part (a).

(c) Identify relevant skill deficiencies.

(d) Identify opportunities to solicit AI system operator feedback.

(e) Identify and address long term technology gaps for the Department's cyber war fighting mission.

(f) Evaluate the risks posed by adversary AI-based attacks.

(g) Furnish a timeline with target milestones, investments, and required expenditures.
 
(h) Incorporate metrics to evaluate AI adoption.

(i) Identify further funding, authorities, and policies that may be necessary.

(j) Define a maturity model describing desired cyber capabilities independent of specific technology solutions.

Casual name: FY2023 NDAA, Section 6731 ("Report on establishment of technology acquisition cadre")

Long summary:
Mandates the Director of National Intelligence to submit a feasibility report on establishing a cadre of experts in emerging technologies.

Requires such cadre to accelerate the adoption of emerging technologies.

Requires such cadre to assist with software development and develop training requirements for acquisition professionals.

Requires an assessment of whether the establishment of the cadre would necessitate additional statutory authorities or resources, including to recruit, hire, and retain the talent and expertise needed to establish the cadre; and the benefits, risks, and costs of establishing a cadre.

Addresses the acquisition of a plan for implementation of the cadre, including the proposed size of the cadre, its management, and organization.

Official name: James M. Inhofe National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2023, Section 162 ("Assessment and strategy for fielding capabilities to counter threats posed by unmanned aerial system swarms")
Casual name: FY2023 NDAA, Section 162 ("Assessment and strategy for fielding capabilities to counter threats posed by unmanned aerial system swarms")
Short summary: Directs the Secretary of Defense to assess the threats and national security implications of unmanned aerial system swarms and review the capabilities of the Defense Department to counter these threats. Directs the Secretary of Defense to develop a strategy to field capabilities to counter threats posed by unmanned aerial system swarms and report the assessment findings and strategy to the congressional defense committees.
Long summary:
Directs the Secretary of Defense to assess threats posed by unmanned aerial system swarms to installations and deployed Armed Forces, the use of these swarms by adversaries and their national security implications such as autonomous intelligence and machine learning, current capabilities of the Defense Department and their effectiveness in countering these threats, and efforts by the Defense Department to develop and field technology that can counter swarms.

Directs the Secretary of Defense to develop and implement a strategy to field capabilities to counter threats from unmanned aerial system swarms. The strategy should define "unmanned aerial system swarm," outline plans for developing, testing, fielding, and acquiring capabilities to counter swarms, estimate resources needed by each Armed Force to implement the strategy, and determine the legislative action necessary for the Defense Department to counter swarm threats.

Directs the Secretary of Defense to consider incorporating the strategy into a comprehensive strategy to defend against unmanned aerial systems and to report the assessment findings and strategy to the congressional defense committees within 270 days.

Short summary: Requires the Secretary of Defense to demonstrate a strategic management dashboard within one year of the Act's enactment. This dashboard will automate data collection and data visualization of the Department of Defense's primary management goals, and will incorporate artificial intelligence and machine learning tools to improve data analytic capabilities and decision making.

Requires the Secretary of Defense to demonstrate a strategic management dashboard within one year of the Act's enactment. Eligible individuals need to be enrolled in a relevant program at an institution of higher education and commit to work for the Department of Defense in an area relevant to the program for a duration equivalent to the duration of the scholarship.

Requires a report to Congress on the success of the program every two years.

Official name: James M. Inhofe National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2023, Section 11504 ("At-sea recovery operations pilot program")
Casual name: FY2023 NDAA, Section 11504 ("At-sea recovery operations pilot program")
Short summary: Requires the Secretary of the Department of Homeland Security to conduct a pilot program to assess the potential for the autonomous, or remotely controlled, operation and monitoring of certain Coast Guard vessels.
Long summary:
Requires the Secretary of the Department of Homeland Security to conduct a pilot program to assess the potential for the autonomous, or remotely controlled, operation and monitoring of certain Coast Guard vessels.

Requires the pilot program described above to assess the complexities and risks of autonomous or remotely controlled vessel operation, gather data on the practice, and assess the regulatory environment necessary to facilitate the practice going forward. 

Permits the pilot program to include operations relating to the launch and recovery of unmanned spaceflight vessels and components.

Permits the Secretary to modify or waive applicable regulations or guidance that would otherwise interfere with the pilot program.

Requires the Secretary to provide the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate and the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure and the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology of the House of Representatives with a quarterly briefing on the pilot program.

Requires the Secretary to submit to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and 
Transportation of the Senate and the Committee on Transportation and 
Infrastructure and the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology of 
the House of Representatives a final report assessing the execution of the pilot program and its implications for preserving safety and environmental quality. 

Requires the Comptroller General of the United States to consult with stakeholders and then submit to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate and the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives a report on the commercial availability, barriers to use, safety and security risks, use abroad, and impact on labor of autonomous and remote technologies used in the operation of shipboard equipment.

Long summary:
Instructs the Administrator of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration to promote the development and use of emerging technologies (e.g., sensors and autonomous vehicles) for research, communication, navigation, and data collection in marine science as part of the Administration's national ocean exploration program.

Casual name: FY2023 NDAA, Section 11225 ("Establishment of unmanned system program and autonomous control and computer vision technology project")
Short summary: Requires the Coast Guard to implement, and brief Congress on, a program for using unmanned vehicles to improve mission execution.
Long summary:
Requires the U.S. Coast Guard to establish a program for using unmanned vehicles -- designed to operate in the air, on the water, and/or below the water's surface --  to improve mission execution. 

Requires the Commandant of the Coast Guard to conduct a project that retrofits at least two small boats with the sensors, communication systems, computer vision systems, and autonomous control technology necessary to conduct unmanned search and rescue, surveillance, and interdiction missions. Requires the Commandant to collect and analyze operational data from this project to inform future requirements. 

Requires the Commandant to submit a project briefing including analysis of operational data and projected costs to the congressional transportation committees.

Short summary: Requires each Secretary of a military department to hold an event every 180 days designed to collaborate with the private sector on, and raise awareness of, innovation in autonomous systems with military applications.
Long summary:
Requires each Secretary of a military department to hold an event every 180 days designed to collaborate with the private sector on, and raise awareness of, innovation in autonomous systems with military applications. 

For each such event, requires the Service Chief concerned to identify military requirements, capability gaps, and opportunities for experimentation or exercises related to the autonomous systems covered at the event. 

For each such event, requires the service acquisition executive of the involved military department to identify acquisition strategies and potential acquisition funding mechanisms for the autonomous systems covered at the event.

Requires Department of Defense officials to draft reviews of each such event, analyze the reviews to identify trends and key takeaways, and distribute the reviews within the Department.

Official name: James M. Inhofe National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2023, Section 6513 ("Lead intelligence community coordinator for countering and neutralizing proliferation of Iran-origin unmanned aircraft systems")
Casual name: FY2023 NDAA, Section 6513 ("Lead intelligence community coordinator for countering and neutralizing proliferation of Iran-origin unmanned aircraft systems")
Short summary: Instructs the Director of National Intelligence to appoint a lead coordinator to address the proliferation of Iran-origin unmanned aircraft systems, develop a comprehensive action plan, and report findings to Congress. Encourages collaboration among members of the Five Eyes Partnership and Israel in countering these threats.
Long summary:
Instructs the Director of National Intelligence to designate an official from the intelligence community to serve as the lead coordinator for countering and neutralizing the proliferation of Iran-origin unmanned aircraft systems.

Requires this Coordinator to develop a comprehensive plan of action, driven by intelligence information, for countering and neutralizing the threats posed by the proliferation of Iran-origin unmanned aircraft systems and submit a briefing of such plans to the Congress.

Requires the Director of National Intelligence to submit a final report on the activities and findings of the Coordinator to the appropriate committees of Congress.

Mandates the report to include an assessment of the threats posed by Iran-origin unmanned aircraft systems and a detailed description of intelligence sharing efforts, as well as other joint efforts driven by intelligence information, with allies and partners of the United States, to assist in countering and neutralizing such threats. 

Mandates the report to include recommendations for any changes in United States policy designed to improve the capacity of the intelligence community to assist in countering such threats.

Encourages collaboration and the expansion of existing cooperative efforts among members of the Five Eyes Partnership and Israel for the purpose of countering Iran-origin unmanned aircraft systems.

Requires the Secretary of State to submit to the appropriate congressional committees a report that describes and justifies any changes made during FY 2022 and 2023 to the Foreign Service entry process.

Includes the use of artificial intelligence, including deep textual analysis, and its impact on recruitment in the scope of the report.

Official name: James M. Inhofe National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2023, Section 6812 ("Increased intelligence-related engineering, research, and development capabilities of minority institutions")
Casual name: FY2023 NDAA, Section 6812 ("Increased intelligence-related engineering, research, and development capabilities of minority institutions")
Short summary: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to develop a plan to promote intelligence-related engineering, research, and development activities at institutes of higher education whose students are predominantly of minority ethnicities.
Long summary:
Requires the Director of National Intelligence to develop and submit to Congress a report and a plan to promote intelligence-related engineering, research, and development activities at institutes of higher education whose students are predominantly of minority ethnicities. 

Requires the plan described above to include an analysis of (a) the opportunities for advancing engineering, research, and development in computer sciences, including artificial intelligence, quantum computing, and machine learning, and synthetic biology, and (b) the investments necessary to do so.

Requires the plan to include metrics of success and actionable recommendations for the Director, Congress, and higher education institutions, and permits the Director to award contracts, grants, or other agreements to support the institutions.

Instructs the Director to establish goals and incentives to encourage the 
Intelligence Advanced Research Projects Activity to develop partnerships between itself, its partner institutions, and institutes of higher education whose students are predominantly of minority ethnicities.

Official name: James M. Inhofe National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2023, Section 6505 ("Assessments of production of semiconductors by the People's Republic of China")
Casual name: FY2023 NDAA, Section 6505 ("Assessments of production of semiconductors by the People's Republic of China")
Short summary: After this act is passed, the Director of National Intelligence must submit a detailed report about the production of semiconductors in China. The Director must also meet with federal agencies' heads to discuss the report and other findings related to the Chinese production of semiconductors.
Long summary:
Begins discussing the phrase “appropriate committees of Congress” and what organizations are actually a part of this phrase when benign referred. Also defines the term “legacy semiconductor” in terms of the definition given in another legislation document.

Annually for 3 years after this act is passed, the Director of National Intelligence must submit an unclassified assessment report regarding China’s production of semiconductors through China’s firms and business sectors to the appropriate committees of Congress.

The Director of National Intelligence must meet with the heads of federal agencies to discuss the elements of the assessment. They must discuss the self-sufficiency of China in the fields of artificial intelligence, cloud computing, autonomous vehicles, and other newer-technological areas. They must discuss China’s access to intellectual property related to leading edge process nodes, including electronic design automation technology. They must discuss Chinese firms’s production of semiconductors that are not legacy conductors and stockpiling efforts of semiconductor manufacturing equipment. They must discuss the relative market share and its trends of Chinese semiconductor manufacturers at different process nodes. They must discuss the outside recruitment activity into Chinese semiconductors firms and the capability of the Chinese workforce in developing semiconductors that are not legacy semiconductors and relevant equipment.

Official name: James M. Inhofe National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2023, Section 6717 ("Policy on required user adoption metrics in certain contracts for artificial intelligence and emerging technology software products")
Casual name: FY2023 NDAA, Section 6717 ("Policy on required user adoption metrics in certain contracts for artificial intelligence and emerging technology software products")
Short summary: Instructs the Director of National Intelligence to establish a policy regarding user adoption metrics for contracts and other agreements for the procurement of AI and emerging technology software products.
Long summary:
Authorizes government support for AI-related activities

Instructs the Director of National Intelligence to establish a policy regarding user adoption metrics for contracts and other agreements for the procurement AI and emerging technology software products.

Requires an evaluation of AI systems, or of broader systems or processes that involve AI.

Requires each contract or agreement to include an understanding of the anticipated use of the covered AI or emerging technology product with a clear metric for success, for collecting user adoption metrics and for assessing the adoption of the covered product by such users.

Requires that prior to the procurement of, or the continuation of the use of, any AI and emerging technology product procured by the head of an element of the intelligence community, the head must determine a method for assessing the success of the covered product from user adoption metrics.

Official name: National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2024, Section 1090 ("Conduct of weather reconnaissance in the United States")
Casual name: FY2024 NDAA, Section 1090 ("Conduct of weather reconnaissance in the United States")
Short summary: Instructs the 53rd Weather Reconnaissance Squadron of the Air Force Reserve Command to use autonomous platform observations to improve water management decision-making and flood forecasting, if the Squadron uses certain available resources.
Long summary:
Requires the Secretary of Commerce to submit to the congressional science committees a report on the resources necessary for the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration to invest in emerging technologies that improve weather forecasting.

Permits the 53rd Weather Reconnaissance Squadron of the Air Force Reserve Command and the Administrator of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration to use aircraft, personnel, and equipment necessary to improve the accuracy and efficacy of weather monitoring and water management, including through advanced technological methods.

Instructs the Squadron to use autonomous platform observations to improve water management decision-making and flood forecasting, if it uses the resources named in the above paragraph.

Requires the Secretary of the Air Force to submit to the appropriate congressional committees a report on the resources used and required by the Air Force and the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration to improve weather monitoring and water management.

Official name: National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2024, Section 346 ("Pilot program on optimization of aerial refueling and fuel management in contested logistics environments through use of artificial intelligence")
Casual name: FY2024 NDAA, Section 346 ("Pilot program on optimization of aerial refueling and fuel management in contested logistics environments through use of artificial intelligence")
Short summary: Mandates the Department of Defense, in partnership with the Air Force, to launch a pilot program to optimize the logistics of aerial refueling and fuel management using  artificial intelligence and advanced digital technologies.
Long summary:
Mandates the Department of Defense and the Air Force to work together in launching a pilot program that optimizes aerial refueling and fuel management using artificial intelligence and advanced digital technologies.

Directs the Chief Digital and Artificial Intelligence Officer to (a) coordinate with the Commander of the United States Transportation Command and the Commander of the United States Indo-Pacific Command to align the pilot program with existing operational requirements and (b) consult relevant experts in AI.

Requires the pilot program to assess the feasibility and effectiveness of artificial intelligence-driven approaches and evaluate the interoperability and compatibility of artificial intelligence-enabled systems with the existing logistics infrastructure of the Department of Defense.

Requires an assessment of potential challenges and risks associated with the integration of artificial intelligence and other advanced digital technologies, including those related to cybersecurity.

Requires the Chief Digital and Artificial Intelligence Officer to submit a report on the pilot program's design, progress, and results to the Committees on Armed Services of the House of Representatives and the Senate within one year.

Official name: National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2024, Section 350 ("Strategy and assessment on use of automation and artificial intelligence for shipyard optimization")
Casual name: FY2024 NDAA, Section 350 ("Strategy and assessment on use of automation and artificial intelligence for shipyard optimization")
Short summary: Requires the Secretary of the Navy to (a) develop a strategy for using artificial intelligence to better predict maintenance needs at U.S. Navy shipyards, and (b) evaluate the potential maintenance cost savings of doing so.
Long summary:
Requires the Secretary of the Navy, in coordination with the Shipyard Infrastructure Optimization Program of the Department of the Navy, to develop a strategy for using artificial intelligence to better predict maintenance needs at U.S. Navy shipyards.

Requires the Secretary of the Navy to evaluate the potential cost savings of adopting artificial intelligence predictive maintenance technologies.

Requires the Secretary of the Navy to brief congressional defense committees on the strategy and evaluation described above.

Official name: National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2024, Section 856 ("Pilot program to analyze and monitor certain supply chains")
Casual name: FY2024 NDAA, Section 856 ("Pilot program to analyze and monitor certain supply chains")
Short summary: Directs the Under Secretary of Defense for Acquisition and Sustainment to establish a pilot program for analyzing supply chains for covered weapons platforms using artificial intelligence and machine learning tools.
Long summary:
Directs the Under Secretary of Defense for Acquisition and Sustainment to establish a pilot program enhancing the analysis, mapping, and monitoring of supply chains for covered weapons platforms using artificial intelligence and machine learning tools.

Requires the Under Secretary to submit an annual report (until 2028) that specifies the vulnerabilities of the supply chains and the efficiencies achieved through using the new tools.

Official name: National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2024, Section 811 ("Modernizing the Department of Defense requirements process")
Casual name: FY2024 NDAA, Section 811 ("Modernizing the Department of Defense requirements process")
Short summary: Requires the Vice Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff to develop a streamlined requirements development process for the Department of Defense; requires  processes for software, artificial intelligence, data, and related capability areas to be more rapid and iterative than the requirements processes for traditional hardware systems. Requires the Secretary of Defense to cooperate  with other military leaders.
Long summary:
Requires the Secretary of Defense in coordination with military leaders to develop streamlined requirements of development processes for the Department of Defense, in order to modernize war fighting technology and reduce the time needed to deliver capabilities to warfighters.

Requires revisions to requirements management practices that maximizes the use of commercial products or services and allow for a broader range of new or alternative technological opportunities to be incorporated without the requirement being validated again.

Establishes a process to rapidly validate the ability of commercial products and services to meet capability needs or opportunities.

Requires retiring and replacing the Department of Defense Architecture Framework with a new structure focused on enabling interoperability  through application program interfaces, enterprise architectures and platforms, and government and commercial standards.

Requires the secretary to develop a formal career path, training, and structure for requirements managers.

Establishes the required processes for software, artificial intelligence, data, and related capability areas to be more rapid and iterative than the requirements processes for traditional hardware systems.

Requires the Vice Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff to collaborate with industry partners, contractors, and Department of Defense science and technology reinvention laboratories in designing the new requirements development process.

Requires the Secretary of Defense to submit to the congressional defense committees an interim report and a final report on the development and implementation of the new requirements development process.

Official name: National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2024, Title XIII, Subtitle B ("Other Matters Relating to Foreign Nations: Matters Relating to the AUKUS Partnership")
Casual name: FY2024 NDAA, Title XIII, Subtitle B ("Other Matters Relating to Foreign Nations: Matters Relating to the AUKUS Partnership")
Short summary: Requires the Secretary of State to (a) submit to Congress a biennial report on efforts to enhance AUKUS collaboration on artificial intelligence and (b) create an expedited technology transfer process for sharing artificial intelligence systems and other defense technologies with AUKUS partners.
Long summary:
Requires the Secretary of State, in consultation with the Secretary of Defense and other agency heads, to submit to Congress a biennial report on the AUKUS partnership, including an assessment of efforts to enhance AUKUS collaboration on artificial intelligence.

Requires the Secretary of State, in consultation with the Secretary of Defense, to create an expedited technology transfer approval process for transferring artificial intelligence systems and other defense technologies to AUKUS partners.

Official name: National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2024, Section 1507 ("Review and plan relating to cyber red teams of Department of Defense")
Casual name: FY2024 NDAA, Section 1507 ("Review and plan relating to cyber red teams of Department of Defense")
Short summary: Requires the Department of Defense to submit to Congress a plan for ensuring that the Department's cyber red teams possess the capabilities necessary to execute their duties, including with respect to threats stemming from artificial intelligence systems abroad.
Long summary:
Requires officials from the Department of Defense -- namely the Principal Cyber Advisor to the Secretary of Defense, the Chief Information Officer of the Department of Defense, the Director of Operational Test and Evaluation, and the Commander of the United States Cyber Command -- to assess the status of the implementation of recommendations made by the Secretary of Defense to enhance the capabilities of the Department of Defense's cyber red teams.

Requires the same officials listed above to submit to Congress a plan for ensuring that the Department's cyber red teams possess the capabilities necessary to execute their duties, including with respect to threats stemming from artificial intelligence systems abroad.

Requires the Director of Operational Test and Evaluation to evaluate annually, from 2025 through 2031, the status of the implementation of the plan described in the paragraph above.

Official name: National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2024, Section 1521 ("Control and management of Department of Defense data; establishment of Chief Digital and Artificial Intelligence Officer Governing Council")
Casual name: FY2024 NDAA, Section 1521 ("Control and management of Department of Defense data; establishment of Chief Digital and Artificial Intelligence Officer Governing Council")
Short summary: Grants the Chief Digital and Artificial Intelligence Officer authority to access and control data on behalf of the Secretary of Defense and establishes a council to oversee the responsible deployment of AI across Department of Defense operations.
Long summary:
Grants the Chief Digital and Artificial Intelligence Officer of the Department of Defense the authority to access and control data on behalf of the Secretary of Defense.

Requires the Secretary to establish the Chief Digital and Artificial Intelligence Officer Governing Council, which will provide policy oversight to ensure the responsible, coordinated, and ethical deployment of AI across Department of Defense missions and operations.

Requires the Council to submit a report on its activities in the past period to the Secretary and Congress at least once every 18 months.

Official name: National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2024, Title XV, Subtitle C ("Cyberspace-Related Matters: Information Technology and Data Management")
Casual name: FY2024 NDAA, Title XV, Subtitle C ("Cyberspace-Related Matters: Information Technology and Data Management")
Short summary: Instructs the Secretary of Defense to establish a council known as the ‘Chief Digital and Artificial Intelligence Officer Governing Council’ to provide policy oversight in order to ensure the responsible, and ethical employment of data and artificial intelligence capabilities across Department of Defense missions; requires any data collection, use, or retention measure to be compliant with applicable laws; establishes a pilot program to assess the feasibility and advisability of implementing industry open technical standards.
Long summary:
Instructs the Secretary of Defense to establish the 'Chief Digital and Artificial Intelligence Officer Governing Council' to oversee policy ensuring responsible and ethical use of data and AI in Department of Defense missions.

Requires  The council, led by the Chief Digital and AI Officer, to develop, implement, and oversee AI, coordinating governance, and providing ethical guidance. 

Instructs the council to identify, monitor, and update recommendations for AI use, review program funding,  and ensure compliance with AI policies. 

Instructs the council to issue guidance on access and distribution restrictions relating to data, models, tool sets, testing or validation infrastructure.

Instructs the council to implement and oversee an educational program on data and artificial intelligence, to familiarize personnel department on the applications of artificial intelligence.

Mandates all data collection, use, or retention measures  to comply with relevant laws and regulations, including those concerning data related to US persons.

Requires an establishment of a course of education at the Defense Information School, the purpose of which shall be to provide instruction on the practical concepts and skills.

Instructs the Director of the Defense Media Activity to carry out a pilot program to assess the feasibility and advisability of implementing industry open technical standards for digital content provenance for official photographs and videos of the Department of Defense

Casual name: FY2024 NDAA, Section 1682 ("Electromagnetic Warfare")
Short summary: Requires several US military branches to assess their electromagnetic spectrum operation abilities, including the need for AI-assisted operations.

Official name: National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2024, Title XV, Subtitle E ("Artificial Intelligence")
Casual name: FY2024 NDAA, Title XV, Subtitle E ("Artificial Intelligence")
Short summary: Directs Secretary of Defense to implement a bug bounty program on DoD AI use and a competition on AI detection and watermarking. Instructs Secretary of Defense to develop guidance on various aspects of AI use within DoD, including bias mitigation, security, and human oversight of autonomous systems, and mandates a study on vulnerabilities of AI in the military.
Long summary:
Updates and extends the DoD's AI and ML acquisition framework and requires a plan for acquisition authority delegation and a demonstration of acquisition authority capabilities.

Mandates the creation of a bug bounty program for foundational AI models within the DoD, promoting collaboration with federal entities and cybersecurity experts. Requires briefing the congressional defense committees on the program within one year.

Instructs the Secretary of Defense to establish a competition to advance technologies for detecting and watermarking generative AI content. Requires annual briefing to the congressional defense committees.

Directs the Secretary of Defense to issue DoD-wide guidance and policy on AI, including with regard to ethical AI use, bias mitigation, cybersecurity, technical workforce assessments, AI literacy needs and related training programs, protection against adversarial AI, protecting the intellectual property of DoD AI system suppliers, AI's potential role in developing offensive actions, availability of inputs for AI, verification and evaluation frameworks, potential vulnerabilities of AI models and human oversight of autonomous systems. Requires briefing the congressional defense committees within 150 days.

Requires a study on the vulnerabilities of AI-enabled military applications, focusing on explainability, security risks, and the integration of decision support systems, with a final report for the congressional defense committees detailing findings and research gaps.

Casual name: FY2024 NDAA, Section 6303 ("Establishment of the Chief Artificial Intelligence Officer of the Department of State")
Short summary: Creates the position of Chief Artificial Intelligence Officer within the Department of State.

Creates the Chief Artificial Intelligence Officer position, who will be responsible for (a) the evaluation and facilitation of responsible AI and machine learning usage and (b) advising the Secretary of State on ethical AI and analytics usage in diplomacy.

Instructs the Chief Artificial Intelligence Officer to partner with the Chief Information Officer on scaling AI use cases.

Official name: National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2024, Section 7510 ("Requirement to ensure intelligence community directives appropriately account for artificial intelligence and machine learning tools in intelligence products")
Casual name: FY2024 NDAA, Section 7510 ("Requirement to ensure intelligence community directives appropriately account for artificial intelligence and machine learning tools in intelligence products")
Short summary: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to brief Congress on whether existing directives provide sufficient guidance on the use of AI in intelligence and, if necessary, on a plan to update the guidance.
Long summary:
Requires the Director of National Intelligence to brief the congressional appropriations and intelligence committees on whether intelligence community directives provide sufficient guidance on how to use, document, and disclose AI and machine learning tools in intelligence. 

Requires the Director to either (a) justify the assertion that existing intelligence community directives do sufficiently provide such guidance or (b) provide a plan and timeline for updating the directives as necessary.

Official name: National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2024, Section 7356 ("Analysis of commercial cloud initiatives of intelligence community")
Casual name: FY2024 NDAA, Section 7356 ("Analysis of commercial cloud initiatives of intelligence community")
Short summary: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to (a) analyze the intelligence community's commercial cloud initiatives -- including their ability to support artificial intelligence capabilities -- and (b) brief the congressional intelligence and appropriations committees on the findings of that analysis.
Long summary:
Requires the Director of National Intelligence to produce a comprehensive analysis of the intelligence community's cloud initiatives relating to the Commercial Cloud Enterprise contract entered into by the Director of the Central Intelligence Agency in November of 2020. 

Requires the above analysis to include projected cloud computing costs, plans for the termination of legacy data storage mechanisms, an assessment of the value and efficacy of cloud computing tools and analytics, and the suitability of existing computing arrangements to service artificial intelligence systems for the intelligence community. 

Requires the Director of National Intelligence to brief the congressional intelligence and appropriations committees on the findings of the analysis detailed above.

Official name: National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2024, Section 112 ("Strategy for Army tactical wheeled vehicle program")
Casual name: FY2024 NDAA, Section 112 ("Strategy for Army tactical wheeled vehicle program")
Short summary:
Requires the Secretary of the Army to submit to Congress a report on the strategy 
of the Army for tactical wheeled vehicles -- including autonomous vehicles -- for FY 2025, 2030, and 2035. The report shall include a description of the force structure and funding necessary for the tactical wheeled vehicle program to support the U.S.'s national security strategy.
Long summary:
Requires the Secretary of the Army to submit to Congress a report on the strategy of the Army for tactical wheeled vehicles, including autonomous vehicles. The report shall be submitted with the budget justification materials submitted in support of the budget of the Department of Defense for each of FY 2025, 2030, and 2035.

Requires the report to include a description of the force structure and funding necessary for the tactical wheeled vehicle program to support the U.S.'s national security strategy, along with detailed rundown of a program for the construction of tactical wheeled vehicles and the estimated cost of such a program. 

Requires the Secretary of the Army to, within 15 days of the budget materials submission described above, brief the congressional defense committees on the investment needed for each platform of tactical wheeled vehicle.

Official name: National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2024, Section 7507 ("Programs for next-generation microelectronics in support of artificial intelligence")
Casual name: FY2024 NDAA, Section 7507 ("Programs for next-generation microelectronics in support of artificial intelligence")
Short summary: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to establish or oversee a program to advance microelectronics research, focused on applications in artificial intelligence and other emerging technologies.
Long summary:
Requires the Director of National Intelligence, acting through the Director of the Intelligence Advanced Research Projects Activity, to establish or oversee a program to advance microelectronics research. 

Requires the program described above to focus on applications in artificial intelligence, advanced computing, advanced computing materials, optical communication networks, quantum technologies, or other emerging technologies. 

Requires the Director of National Intelligence to consider a variety of factors when crafting the research program, including input from the Secretary of Commerce and from an array of stakeholders including representatives of industry and academia.

Requires the Director of the Intelligence Advanced Research Projects Activity to brief the congressional intelligence, appropriations, and commerce committees on the progress of the research program described above and recommendations for future research priorities.

Casual name: FY2024 NDAA, Section 7513 ("Policies established by Director of National Intelligence for artificial intelligence capabilities")
Short summary: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to implement policies for the third-party acquisition, development, and use of artificial intelligence capabilities.
Long summary:
Instructs the Director of National Intelligence, in consultation with the Director of the Office of Management and Budget and other intelligence officials to maintain, develop, and annually revise various AI policies.

Requires the establishment of a lexicon relating to machine learning and AI use by the intelligence community.

Requires the establishment of guidelines for evaluating model performance by specifying conditions for AI capability monitoring, documenting performance objectives, specifying model auditing processes, and specifying conditions for vulnerability testing and privacy evaluations.

Requires the establishment of guidelines for tracking dependencies in related systems, documentation requirements for capabilities procured from third-party and training data usage, and guidance on the usage of third-party-trained models.

Casual name: FY2024 NDAA, Section 903 ("Establishment of Office of Strategic Capital")
Short summary: Creates a new Office of Strategic Capital in the Department of Defense to invest in companies developing advanced technologies, including autonomous robots, that may benefit the Department of Defense.
Long summary:
Creates a new Office of Strategic Capital in the Department of Defense.

Directs the new Office to invest in companies developing critical technologies, including autonomous robots, that may benefit the Department of Defense. 

Permits the Office to launch a pilot program for providing capital assistance that supports the defense industrial base. 

Requires the Office's investments to meet certain criteria, including restrictions on interest rates and loan repayment periods as well as requirements for non-Federal investments in selected companies, subject to various exceptions. 

Requires the Secretary of Defense to submit to the congressional defense committees an annual report on the Office's activities, in addition to notifying the congressional defense committees whenever it provides capital assistance.

Casual name: FY2024 NDAA, Section 7511 ("Expanded annual assessment of economic and technological capabilities of the People's Republic of China and related briefing")

Long summary:
Instructs the Director of National Intelligence to provide a briefing to the congressional intelligence committees on the status and implementation of working groups on the People's Republic of China's activities regarding certain technologies, including artificial intelligence. 

Instructs working groups to assess the investments made by the People's Republic of China in AI, the competitive practices of the People's Republic of China relating to AI, opportunities to counter those practices, and US vulnerabilities in the supply chain of AI.

Official name: National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2024, Section 224 ("Next Generation Air Dominance family of systems development program accountability matrices")
Casual name: FY2024 NDAA, Section 224 ("Next Generation Air Dominance family of systems development program accountability matrices")
Short summary:
Submit matrices that detail engineering, manufacturing, and development for Next Generation Air Dominance and Collaborative Combat Aircraft programs to congressional defense committees as well as the Comptroller General. 

Provide updates on progress and cost estimates every 6 months.

Develops key performance parameters for the program and its objectives to make sure there is transparency and oversight within the system.
Long summary:
Submit goals for Next Generation Air Dominance and Collaborative Combat Aircraft programs to congressional defense committees and the Comptroller General concurrent with the President's budget submission. Include key milestones, testing events, and performance goals, subdivided into six-month increments. 

Provide information on technology readiness levels, design maturity, software maturity, integration maturity, manufacturing readiness levels, reliability, availability for flight operations, and maintainability.

Provide updates on progress and cost estimates for the program every 6 months. It details progress made toward the goals identified in the matrices and provides updated cost estimates for air vehicles, propulsion, mission systems, software, program management, system test and evaluation, support and training systems, and more.

Develop key performance parameters (cost KPPs) for cost thresholds and objectives for the programs. Specify threshold and objective values for categories such as unit recurring flyaway cost, average procurement unit cost, gross/weapon system unit cost, aircraft cost-per-tail-per-year, and aircraft cost-per-flight-hour. 

Ensure that each Secretary concerned works on these parameters for the respective programs and includes them in the related documents.

Ensure assessment of the sufficiency of matrices by the Comptroller General, who will review them and submit assessments to congressional defense committees, identifying any cost, schedule, or performance trends.

Official name: National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2024, Section 253 ("Annual review of status of implementation plan for digital engineering career tracks")
Casual name: FY2024 NDAA, Section 253 ("Annual review of status of implementation plan for digital engineering career tracks")
Short summary: Requires an annual report on the Department of Defense's digital engineer career track plan by the Secretary of Defense to Congress, including an assessment of how advances in machine learning affect the plan.
Long summary:
Requires the Secretary of Defense to conduct annually, through 2029, an internal review of the Department of Defense's plan for the digital engineer career track. The review shall include consideration of how the rapid rate of technological change in data science and machine learning may affect the implementation of the plan.

Requires the Secretary to submit annually, through 2029, a report to the congressional defense committees on the findings of that internal review. The report shall include the Secretary's plan for addressing the digital engineer personnel needs of the Department.

Casual name: FY2025 NDAA, Section 1547 ("Joint partner-sharing network capabilities for Middle East defense integration")
Short summary: Obliges the Chief Data and Artificial Intelligence Officer to develop policies for a combatant command warfighter forum on AI, considering purposes such as AI tool use cases, risk categorization, and training needs.
Long summary:
Obliges the Chief Data and Artificial Intelligence Officer to develop policies for a combatant command warfighter forum on AI, considering purposes such as AI tool use cases, risk categorization, and training needs.

Demands identification of opportunities for AI cooperation with regional partners and allies.

Directs the Secretary of Defense to submit a report to congressional defense committees detailing the initiatives, challenges, and policy needs for a combatant command warfighter forum on AI.

Official name: Servicemember Quality of Life Improvement and National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2025, Section 6504 ("Establishment of Artificial Intelligence Security Center")
Casual name: FY2025 NDAA, Section 6504 ("Establishment of Artificial Intelligence Security Center")
Short summary: Establishes the Artificial Intelligence Security Center within the Cybersecurity Collaboration Center of NSA to develop guidance against counter-AI techniques and promote secure AI adoption practices.
Long summary:
Mandates the Director of the National Security Agency to establish an Artificial Intelligence Security Center (within the Cybersecurity Collaboration Center of NSA) within 90 days of the Act's enactment.

Requires the Center to develop guidance to prevent or mitigate counter-artificial intelligence techniques.

Promotes secure artificial intelligence adoption practices for managers of national security systems and the defense industrial base.

Official name: Servicemember Quality of Life Improvement and National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2025, Section 1638 ("Sense of Congress with respect to use of artificial intelligence to support strategic deterrence")
Casual name: FY2025 NDAA, Section 1638 ("Sense of Congress with respect to use of artificial intelligence to support strategic deterrence")
Short summary: Declares that it is the policy of the United States to ensure AI use does not undermine the integrity of nuclear safeguards or human decision-making with respect to the use of nuclear weapons.
Long summary:
Expresses the sense of Congress that artificial intelligence and machine learning can strengthen strategic communications, improve planning efficiency, and enhance modeling capabilities for stockpile stewardship.

Emphasizes the need for caution to prevent AI from compromising critical strategic assets.

Establishes U.S. policy that AI use should not undermine nuclear safeguards, affect weapon system functionality, or alter the validation of command communications.

Requires positive human actions for nuclear weapon employment decisions by the President to remain intact.

Official name: Servicemember Quality of Life Improvement and National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2025, Section 6506 ("Enhancement of authority for intelligence community public-private talent exchanges")
Casual name: FY2025 NDAA, Section 6506 ("Enhancement of authority for intelligence community public-private talent exchanges")
Short summary: Amends the Intelligence Authorization Act to enhance intelligence community-private sector talent exchanges, focusing on fields like AI.
Long summary:
Amends section 5306 to require public-private talent exchanges in intelligence or counterintelligence to focus on fields including artificial intelligence.

Extends the duration of temporary details for such exchanges from 3 years to 5 years.

Prohibits private-sector employees detailed to the intelligence community from accessing trade secrets or proprietary information.

Clarifies that participation in the exchange program does not create an organizational conflict of interest solely by virtue of involvement.

Requires implementation of systems to address any identified conflicts of interest.

Mandates the Director of National Intelligence to submit annual reports to Congress on the implementation and use of the exchange program, including recommendations for improvements, for three consecutive years.

Official name: Servicemember Quality of Life Improvement and National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2025, Section 1078 ("Study and report on Department of Defense use of unmanned ground vehicle systems manufactured by certain foreign entities")
Casual name: FY2025 NDAA, Section 1078 ("Study and report on Department of Defense use of unmanned ground vehicle systems manufactured by certain foreign entities")
Short summary: Requires the Secretary of Defense to study and report on the use of unmanned ground vehicle systems produced by specified foreign adversaries, and prohibits procuring the systems if a national security threat is found.
Long summary:
Mandates the Secretary of Defense to study the use of unmanned ground vehicle systems by the Department of Defense manufactured by certain foreign entities.

Requires submission of a report to congressional defense committees within 180 days, assessing usage, national security threats, and identifying foreign manufacturers.

Calls for the evaluation of cybersecurity risks, technological vulnerabilities, and the foreign entities' military-civil nexus.

Demands an assessment of the feasibility of the Defense Innovation Unit developing a list of U.S. manufacturers.

Mandates an evaluation of whether a prohibition on procuring and operating these systems is advisable for U.S. national security.

Prohibits procurement and operation of these systems by the Department of Defense one year after the report submission, unless deemed in the national interest.

Exempts this prohibition if recommended as not in the best national security interest.

Defines "covered foreign countries" including China, Russia, Iran, and North Korea.

Official name: Servicemember Quality of Life Improvement and National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2025, Section 1069 ("Biodefense posture reviews")
Casual name: FY2025 NDAA, Section 1069 ("Biodefense posture reviews")
Short summary: Directs the Secretary of Defense and requires a review of methods to protect against biological attacks using AI and related topics.
Long summary:
Requires the Secretary of Defense to conduct comprehensive biodefense posture reviews by December 31, 2026, and December 31, 2029.

Mandates inclusion of an inventory and assessment of existing biodefense strategies and policies, relevant biological threats, and current Department of Defense programs related to biological threats.

Identifies methods using artificial intelligence and cyber capabilities to address biological attacks.

Recommends strengthening biodefense capabilities and improving interagency coordination.

Requires submittal of a review to congressional defense committees within 30 days of completion.

Specifies that reviews must be unclassified but may include a classified annex.

Official name: Servicemember Quality of Life Improvement and National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2025, Section 1515 ("Protective measures for mobile devices within the Department of Defense")
Casual name: FY2025 NDAA, Section 1515 ("Protective measures for mobile devices within the Department of Defense")
Short summary: Orders an evaluation of potential protective measures for mobile devices within the Department of Defense, including defenses against AI-driven smishing and phishing.
Long summary:
Requires the Secretary of Defense to evaluate cybersecurity products and services for mobile devices to enhance the Department of Defense's cybersecurity.

Requires the Secretary to evaluate technologies such as anonymizing-enabling technologies, network-enabled content inspection, on-device VPNs, and AI-based protection against smishing, phishing, and text/email compromises.

Requires the Secretary to assess the efficacy and scalability of these technologies within the Department of Defense and their integration with existing cybersecurity architecture.

Mandates submission of a report to congressional defense committees within 270 days detailing findings and recommendations on procuring or incorporating evaluated technologies.

Official name: Servicemember Quality of Life Improvement and National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2025, Section 1066 ("Reports on approval and deployment of lethal autonomous weapon systems")
Casual name: FY2025 NDAA, Section 1066 ("Reports on approval and deployment of lethal autonomous weapon systems")
Short summary: Requires the Secretary of Defense to submit annual reports on DoD approvals and deployments of lethal autonomous weapon systems to the congressional defense committees.
Long summary:
Mandates the Secretary of Defense to submit annual reports to congressional defense committees on lethal autonomous weapon systems by December 31, 2025, and annually until December 31, 2029.

Requires inclusion of a comprehensive list of lethal autonomous weapon systems approved for use, waived from review, under review, and not approved, along with relevant dates.

Specifies that the initial report should cover all relevant periods as determined by the Secretary, while subsequent reports cover periods since the last report.

Allows for reports to be unclassified but permits a classified annex.

Official name: Servicemember Quality of Life Improvement and National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2025, Section 1032 ("Competitive demonstration of large and extra large unmanned underwater vehicles")
Casual name: FY2025 NDAA, Section 1032 ("Competitive demonstration of large and extra large unmanned underwater vehicles")
Short summary: Requires the Navy to demonstrate large and extra large unmanned underwater vehicles by June 2025 and conduct related assessments.
Long summary:
Mandates the Secretary of the Navy to conduct a competitive demonstration of large and extra large unmanned underwater vehicles by June 1, 2025, in coordination with relevant military entities.

Requires evaluation of user feedback on these vehicles as well as vehicles' integration with mission autonomy planning, capability in contested environments, performance attributes, technical maturity, and cost to procure, operate, and sustain.

Permits the use of fiscal year 2025 Navy funds for research, development, and demonstration activities.

Obligates the Secretary of the Navy and the Commander of the United States Indo-Pacific Command to submit assessments to congressional defense committees by September 1, 2025, which may include recommendations and validation of underwater vehicle requirements.

Defines the vehicles as capable of submerged operation while supporting one or more missions with modular payload integration and a minimum range of 1,000 nautical miles.

Official name: Servicemember Quality of Life Improvement and National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2025, Section Title XV, Subtitle D ("Artificial Intelligence")
Casual name: FY2025 NDAA, Title XV, Subtitle D ("Artificial Intelligence")
Short summary: Eestablishes an initiative to improve the usability of AI systems through human factors, an expansion plan for high-performance computing infrastructure, a cost budgeting plan for data used in AI, and a report on the feasibility and advisability of a center(s) for AI-enabled weapon systems.
Long summary:
Establishes an initiative to improve the usability of AI systems, that are used by Department of Defense personnel, by considering human factors and cognitive ergonomics. It mandates the development of a guidance plan for their inclusion and evaluation in current and future programs. 

Establishes a program for high-performance computing infrastructure to support advanced AI capabilities, ensuring non-duplication with Department of Energy efforts. The instrument mandates the development of a roadmap that assesses the anticipated compute requirements, security standards, and relevant resources for the infrastructure required to enable advanced AI capabilities. 

Mandates developing a plan for costs and budgeting related to data used for various stages in the AI development and deployment lifecycle. This plan must include budgets for current programs and programs established by the enactment of this Act, as well as, costs related to data procurement, preparation, protection, and analysis. 

Requires the Secretary of Defense to evaluate the feasibility of centers of excellence for AI-enabled weapon systems, focusing on national and international collaboration, training of personnel, and best practices. Requires a report and briefing to Congress.

Official name: Servicemember Quality of Life Improvement and National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2025, Section 1007 ("Use of technology using artificial intelligence to facilitate audit of the financial statements of the Department of Defense for fiscal year 2025")
Casual name: FY2025 NDAA, Section 1007 ("Use of technology using artificial intelligence to facilitate audit of the financial statements of the Department of Defense for fiscal year 2025")
Short summary: Encourages the use of AI and machine learning to facilitate Department of Defense financial audits.
Long summary:
Encourages the Secretaries of Defense, Army, Navy, and Air Force to use AI and machine learning technologies to facilitate audits of the Department of Defense's financial statements for fiscal year 2025.

Requires the Director of the Chief Digital and Artificial Intelligence Office, in coordination with the Under Secretary of Defense for Research and Engineering and the Inspector General, to oversee the adoption of AI and machine learning technologies for financial management and enterprise business operations.

Official name: Servicemember Quality of Life Improvement and National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2025, Section 1031 ("Requirements for the unmanned maritime autonomy architecture")
Casual name: FY2025 NDAA, Section 1031 ("Requirements for the unmanned maritime autonomy architecture")
Short summary: Provides requirements for the unmanned maritime autonomy architecture that the Secretary of the Navy must implement within 180 days, including providing a forum and resources to facilitate industry participation in the creation and management of a modular open systems architecture for maritime unmanned systems.
Long summary:
Requires, within 180 days, the Secretary of the Navy to provide a forum and resources for industry participation in developing a vendor-agnostic and platform-agnostic modular open systems architecture for maritime unmanned systems.

Directs the Secretary of the Navy to adopt or join a governance model that includes both Government and industry for the maritime unmanned systems standards.

Mandates the implementation of a process for incorporating industry feedback into these standards and aligning them with leading industry practices.

Requires the standards for maritime unmanned systems to be tailored for each relevant Navy program or contract to meet the minimum standards necessary for desired operational capabilities.

Instructs the Secretary of the Navy to label and distribute the standards as open and publicly releasable information to the greatest extent possible.

Official name: Servicemember Quality of Life Improvement and National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2025, Section 1087 ("Establishment of Department of Defense working group on multilateral artificial intelligence coordination")
Casual name: FY2025 NDAA, Section 1087 ("Establishment of Department of Defense working group on multilateral artificial intelligence coordination")
Short summary: Demands the Secretary of Defense to establish a Department of Defense working group to coordinate AI initiatives with allies.
Long summary:
Demands the Secretary of Defense to establish a Department of Defense working group to coordinate AI initiatives with U.S. allies and partners within 90 days of the Act's enactment.

Designates a senior civilian or military officer to lead the group and determines allied participation.

Requires the group to develop AI strategies and interoperability for intelligence sharing, battlespace awareness, and other defense operations.

Mandates managing AI data security and evaluating defense industrial capabilities for AI integration.

Ensures knowledge and technical data remain under the control of the originating country’s export laws.

Terminates the group on September 30, 2028, unless extended for national security reasons by the Secretary.

Defines key terms such as "battlespace awareness" and "covered operational use."

Official name: Servicemember Quality of Life Improvement and National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2025, Section 237 ("Pilot program on use of artificial intelligence for certain workflow and operations tasks")
Casual name: FY2025 NDAA, Section 237 ("Pilot program on use of artificial intelligence for certain workflow and operations tasks")
Short summary: Requires the Secretary of Defense to initiate a pilot program using AI-enabled software to optimize specified Department of Defense workflows and operations tasks.
Long summary:
Requires the Secretary of Defense to initiate a pilot program within 60 days to evaluate AI-enabled software for optimizing workflows and operations at Department of Defense facilities, including depots and shipyards, and in contract administration.

Mandates the use of top-tier software platforms, consideration of industry best practices, implementation based on human-centered design, and connection to authoritative enterprise data sources.

Obliges consultation with the Under Secretary of Defense for Acquisition and Sustainment, and the Secretaries of the Army, Navy, and Air Force.

Requires a report within one year detailing software evaluations, workflow modifications, and a quantitative assessment of the software's impact.

Official name: Servicemember Quality of Life Improvement and National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2025, Section 236 ("Pilot program on development of near-term use cases and demonstration of artificial intelligence toward biotechnology applications for national security")
Casual name: FY2025 NDAA, Section 236 ("Pilot program on development of near-term use cases and demonstration of artificial intelligence toward biotechnology applications for national security")
Short summary: Requires the Secretary of Defense to conduct a pilot program for developing AI for national security-related biotechnology applications through public-private partnerships.
Long summary:
Mandates the Secretary of Defense to implement a pilot program demonstrating artificial intelligence in national security-related biotechnology applications.

Requires collaboration through public-private partnerships to execute the program.

Permits the use of Department of Defense laboratory facilities and computing infrastructure to support biotechnology experimentation and AI model validation.

Stipulates the program's commencement within one year of enactment and its termination five years after starting.

Obligates the Secretary to submit annual reports to congressional defense committees, detailing program progress, data resource assessments, cybersecurity measures, collaboration mechanisms, and AI roles in biotechnology.

Requires a transition plan to be submitted one year before program termination, outlining steps for operationalization, including costs, infrastructure needs, and ecosystem analysis.

Aims to assess the viability of transitioning technology developed in the pilot into operational use within the Department.

Casual name: FY2025 NDAA, Section 1514 ("Management and cybersecurity of multi-cloud environments")
Short summary: Mandates the Secretary of Defense to develop a strategy for the management and cybersecurity of the DOD's multi-cloud environments, including assessing means to increase AI use in these multi-cloud environments and identifying policies for protecting government data, and brief the congressional defense committees on such a strategy.
Long summary:
Mandates the Secretary of Defense to develop a multi-cloud management and cybersecurity strategy within 180 days.

Requires the strategy to assess means to increase AI adoption in the DOD's multi-cloud environments and identify opportunities to improve planning on data use and storage, including policies to enforce the protection of government data used for training AI models.

Directs the DOD's Chief Information Officer to submit the strategy and brief congressional defense committees within 240 days.

Official name: Servicemember Quality of Life Improvement and National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2025, Section 221 ("Improvements relating to defining, identifying, and planning the artificial intelligence workforce of the Department of Defense")
Casual name: FY2025 NDAA, Section 221 ("Improvements relating to defining, identifying, and planning the artificial intelligence workforce of the Department of Defense")
Short summary: Requires the Secretary of Dense to appoint a 'Chief Digital Engineering Recruitment and Management Officer of the Department of Defense' responsible for AI workforce policy and planning.
Long summary:
Appoints a civilian official as the Chief Digital Engineering Recruitment and Management Officer by April 30, 2025, responsible for AI workforce policy and planning in the Department of Defense.

Requires the Officer to define and identify the AI workforce, clarify roles, code roles in workforce data systems, and develop qualification programs.

Mandates updating the Department's Human Capital Operating Plan to align with strategic goals and ensure consistent AI terminology.

Sets the expiration of the Officer's appointment on September 30, 2030.

Demands a briefing to congressional defense committees within 240 days, detailing AI workforce positions, required skills, current workforce state, and future requirements.

Casual name: FY2025 NDAA, Section 235 ("Competitive demonstration of automated target recognition algorithms")
Short summary: Requires the Chief Digital and AI Officer to develop a venue and testing processes for comparing automated target recognition algorithms by June 1, 2025. Mandates testing of Replicator algorithms by September 1, 2025.
Long summary:
Requires the Chief Digital and Artificial Intelligence Officer of the Department of Defense to establish a venue and processes for testing automated target recognition algorithms by June 1, 2025.

Mandate the testing of mission capability for at least two programs in the Replicator initiative using the developed venue by September 1, 2025.

Oblige the Secretary of Defense to brief the Senate and House Armed Services Committees on activities under this section within one year of the Act's enactment.

Casual name: FY2025 NDAA, Section 125 ("Designation of official responsible for autonomous surface and underwater dual-modality vehicles")
Short summary: Requires the Secretary of the Navy to designate an official responsible for developing and acquiring surface and underwater autonomous vehicles.
Long summary:
Requires the Secretary of the Navy to designate an official responsible for developing and acquiring advanced autonomous surface and underwater vehicles within 180 days of enactment.

Mandates the inclusion of a dedicated program element in the Navy's budget for the development and acquisition of these autonomous vehicles.

Official name: Servicemember Quality of Life Improvement and National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2025, Section 225 ("Duties of Chief Digital and Artificial Intelligence Officer Governing Council relating to artificial intelligence models and advanced artificial intelligence technologies")
Casual name: FY2025 NDAA, Section 225 ("Duties of Chief Digital and Artificial Intelligence Officer Governing Council relating to artificial intelligence models and advanced artificial intelligence technologies")
Short summary: Amends the duties of the Chief Digital and Artificial Intelligence Officer Governing Council to assess AI models for national security risks, develop strategies against unauthorized access by adversaries, and recommend legislative or administrative actions on AI to Congress and agencies.
Long summary:
Amends the John S. McCain National Defense Authorization Act to include duties for the Chief Digital and Artificial Intelligence Officer Governing Council.

Requires the Chief Digital and Artificial Intelligence Officer Governing Counci to evaluate and assesses AI models and technologies that could pose national security risks if accessed by U.S. adversaries.

Calls for the development of strategies to prevent unauthorized access and use of powerful AI models by adversarial countries.
