Official name: Servicemember Quality of Life Improvement and National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2025, Section 222 ("Modification to artificial intelligence education strategy")
Casual name: FY2025 NDAA, Section 222 ("Modification to artificial intelligence education strategy")
Short summary: Requires the development of distance education courses on AI and ML concepts, ethics, and applications, for Armed Forces members.
Long summary:
Amends Section 256 of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2020 to mandate the development of AI and machine learning education platforms.

Requires the Chief Digital and Artificial Intelligence Officer of the Department of Defense, in coordination with the Under Secretary of Defense for Personnel and Readiness, to create distance education courses on foundational AI and machine learning concepts and their ethical and responsible use.

Mandates making these courses available to Armed Forces members within 180 days of the enactment of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2025.

Requires the Secretary of Defense to report to congressional defense committees on the progress of implementing this educational initiative within 270 days of the subsection's enactment.

Official name: Servicemember Quality of Life Improvement and National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2025, Section 233 ("Management and utilization of digital data to enhance maintenance activities")
Casual name: FY2025 NDAA, Section 233 ("Management and utilization of digital data to enhance maintenance activities")
Short summary: Requires development policies to leverage DOD operational data for military maintenance using AI and automated systems.
Long summary:
Mandates the development and implementation of policies to manage and utilize digital data for maintenance of aircraft, ships, and ground vehicles within one year.

Requires investment in advanced data infrastructure for efficient data processing.

Instructs the development of a strategy incorporating AI and automated systems for data organization.

Demands collaboration with vendors to overcome proprietary data limitations through open data approaches.

Addresses enhancement of data transmission capabilities with high-speed technologies and secure methods.

Mandates central compilation of maintenance data and creation of user interfaces for data analysis.

Requires vendor-agnostic, interoperable tagging systems unless exceptions are justified.

Calls for review of classification policies to ensure appropriate classification without limiting usability.

Establishes protocols for detecting unauthorized access to vehicle or platform systems.

Obligates a briefing to Senate and House Armed Services Committees on policy development and implementation status.

Casual name: FY2025 NDAA, Section 232 ("Expansion of participation in the Digital On-Demand Program")
Short summary: Requires the Secretary of Defense to expand and promote the Digital On-Demand education program across the Department of Defense.
Long summary:
Mandates the Secretary of Defense to expand the Digital On-Demand Program to all organizations and elements, Armed Forces members, and civilian employees within the Department of Defense.

Requires active promotion of the Program throughout the Department.

Obligates the Secretary of Defense to submit a progress report to the Senate and House Armed Services Committees within 180 days of enactment and annually through 2029.

Defines the Digital On-Demand Program as an initiative overseen by the Chief Digital and Artificial Intelligence Officer, providing on-demand educational resources on AI, emerging technologies, and data literacy to Department of Defense personnel via a digital platform.

Official name: Open Translation Center Act of 2024
Casual name: Open Translation Center Act
Short summary: Establishes the Open Translation Center to translate and analyze documents from designated countries using AI and other technologies. Requires a board to oversee, appoints members from relevant government agencies, and mandates cooperation with U.S. and foreign entities.
Long summary:
Establishes the Open Translation Center (OTC) to translate and analyze foreign language materials from designated countries for public dissemination, enhancing understanding of foreign governments, economies, and cultures.

Administers OTC either as a separate entity or a nonprofit and requires a governing board of five appointed members with relevant expertise, independent of the U.S. Government.

Tasks OTC with translating documents into English, providing analyses, and making materials publicly available while respecting copyright laws.

Requests OTC to use software, programs, artificial intelligence, and other technologies to ensure timely translations.

Requires cooperation with federal agencies and stakeholders, and authorizes consultation with foreign governments after certification by the Secretary of State.

Designates the Secretary of State as the primary sponsor, with potential sponsorship from other federal agencies such as the CIA.

Allocates funding of $85 million for fiscal year 2024 and $80 million for subsequent years through 2028 for OTC operations.
Tags: Strategies: New institution;Strategies: Convening;Strategies: Convening;Applications: Government: other applications/unspecified;Strategies: Convening

Official name: Arkansas SB 656
Casual name: Arkansas SB 656 ("Data-Driven Decision-Making Task Force")
Short summary: Establishes a task force composed of twelve legislative members to study and recommend a shared services model for statewide data sharing, including necessary legislation and funding mechanisms.
Long summary:
Creates the Data-Sharing and Data-Driven Decision-Making Task Force, comprised of twelve members appointed from the Senate and House of Representatives.

Mandates the task force to study and recommend the implementation of a statewide data-sharing system across state agencies.

Requires the task force to evaluate current data management inefficiencies, and propose necessary legislation and funding mechanisms for system implementation. Empowers the task force to hire external consultants if necessary.

Requires the task force to meet at least monthly and report findings to the Governor and legislative leaders by December 31, 2019; expires the task force on January 1, 2020.
Tags: Strategies: New institution;Strategies: Convening;Strategies: Evaluation;Strategies: Government study or report;Strategies: Convening;Strategies: Government support;Applications: Government: benefits and welfare;Applications: Government: other applications/unspecified

Official name: Advanced Weather Model Computing Development Act
Casual name: Advanced Weather Model Computing Development Act
Short summary: Directs the Department of Energy and NOAA to collaborate on AI and high-performance computing for climate and weather modeling. Establishes competitive processes, data sharing, and infrastructure improvements, with progress reports required two years post-enactment. Termination occurs five years post-enactment.
Long summary:
Directs the Department of Energy (DOE) and the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) to collaborate on AI and high-performance computing research to improve climate models and weather prediction.

Requires the establishment of a memorandum of understanding to facilitate this collaboration through a competitive, merit-reviewed process.

Authorizes the Secretary of Energy and NOAA Administrator to conduct research on modeling, simulation, machine learning, data assimilation, and predictive analysis techniques.

Mandates the promotion of collaboration and data sharing among federal agencies, National Laboratories, educational institutions, and nonprofits, while ensuring compliance with national security policies.

Instructs the submission of a report within two years detailing interagency coordination, research achievements, and future opportunities.

Initiates a high-performance computing project to enhance weather prediction models, requiring a comparative analysis with current forecasts.

Sunsets the authority for this initiative five years post-enactment.

Ensures compliance with research security provisions under the CHIPS Act of 2022.
Tags: Strategies: Government support;Strategies: Government support: For R&D;Strategies: Convening;Strategies: Government study or report;Strategies: Governance development;Strategies: Convening;Strategies: Government support;Strategies: Government support: For R&D;Strategies: Evaluation;Strategies: Governance development

Official name: New Mexico SM 63
Casual name: New Mexico SM 63 ("Structured Decision-making in Child Abuse")
Short summary: Requests the attorney general to form a task force to evaluate the use and effectiveness of AI-based structured decision-making tools by New Mexico's Children, Youth and Families Department, involving stakeholder input, with findings due by August 1, 2024.
Long summary:
Requests the Attorney General to convene a task force to study the Children, Youth, and Families Department's use of structured decision-making tools and philosophy.

Mandates the task force to assess the efficacy, impact, and results of these AI-based tools.

Requires the task force to determine if structured decision-making tools lead to better decisions in child abuse and neglect cases.

Directs the task force to analyze staff training, understanding, and data on repeat maltreatment rates since the tool’s introduction.

Instructs the task force to review the outcomes of voluntary placement agreements and safety plans under this new philosophy.

Obligates the task force to make recommendations for improvements and the future use of these tools.

Involves input from diverse stakeholders, including experts in law enforcement, child maltreatment, behavioral health, and representatives from various departments and communities.

Requires collaboration with a post-secondary institution for research.

Demands findings and recommendations be presented to the legislative health and human services committee by August 1, 2024.

Official name: Department of State Authorization Act of 2023, Sec. 931 ("Priority for Australia and the United Kingdom in Foreign Military Sales and Direct Commercial Sales")
Casual name: Department of State Authorization Act, Sec. 931 ("Priority for Australia and UK in Military Sales")
Short summary: Establishes expedited processing for AI technology transfers to Australia, the UK, and Canada under AUKUS. Requires anticipatory release policy for AI and related technologies in military sales. Mandates interagency review and update of related policies and guidance.
Long summary:
Requires the President to institute expedited policies for defense article and service requests from Australia and the United Kingdom under the AUKUS partnership.

Mandates prioritization of these requests over all others, except those from Taiwan and Ukraine.

Instructs the Secretary, in consultation with the Secretary of Defense, to establish an anticipatory release policy for technology transfers to Australia, the UK, and Canada.

Specifies the inclusion of technologies related to submarines, combat systems, hypersonic missiles, cyber capabilities, artificial intelligence, quantum technologies, and other advanced technologies.

Subjects technology transfer reviews to an expedited decision-making process.

Directs the Secretary and the Secretary of Defense to update interagency policies and guidance to incorporate these anticipatory release provisions.

Official name: Promoting Precision Agriculture Act of 2023
Casual name: Promoting Precision Agriculture Act of 2023
Short summary: Requires the secretary to work with the Director of the National Institute of Standard and Technology and the Federal Communication Commission to develop percussion of agriculture and practices to approach new precision agriculture with private company standards. Mandates a study every 2 years and then the following 8 years to ensure these standards are up to date. These studies are voluntary and the coordination with the consensus standard is voluntary.
Long summary:
Requires the Secretary of Agriculture, to coordinate with the National Institute of Standards and Technology and the Federal Communications Commission to advance on Precision Agriculture.

Focuses on, Global positioning system, satellites, yield monitors, soil mapping, sensors for gathering data on crops, internet and other forms of technology. 

Requires these standards to address the evolving demands and connectivity needs of precision agriculture and consider cybersecurity challenges and the impact of AI and advanced wireless technology. Including the demands of growing need for precision agriculture in the US. 

Ensure that this technology has the support of these offices by conducting repeated studies that are up to the private companies standards. 

Mandates a study every 2 years and then the following 8 years to ensure these standards are up to date. These studies are voluntary and the coordination with the consensus standard is voluntary.
Tags: Applications: Agriculture and resource extraction

Official name: Solving the Border Crisis Act
Casual name: Solving the Border Crisis Act
Short summary: Resumes border wall construction and requires compliance with DNA collection laws. Disapproves rule change on asylum claims. Amends treatment for aliens from contiguous territories to ensure return or detention. Sets minimum staffing levels for immigration enforcement and detention facilities funding.
Long summary:
Resumes border wall system construction, requiring the Secretary of Homeland Security to restart halted projects and honor previous agreements.

Prohibits the cancellation of contracts related to border wall construction entered into before January 20, 2021.

Requires the lifting of COVID-19 related suspension of entries and imports not earlier than 120 days after the termination of the public health emergency.

Mandates submission of plans to manage possible influxes related to the termination of COVID-19 suspensions.

Disapproves the rule allowing asylum officers to adjudicate credible fear claims, maintaining exclusive authority for immigration judges.

Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to require the return of aliens to contiguous territory or detain them for further asylum consideration.

Establishes minimum staffing levels for various immigration and border enforcement offices, including 25,000 U.S. Border Patrol agents.

Ensures funding for sufficient detention facilities to comply with mandatory detention requirements.

Mandates a report within 60 days on how the Department of Homeland Security is mitigating border encounters.

Official name: National Strategy to Utilize Microreactors for Natural Disaster Response Efforts Act
Casual name: Microreactors for Natural Disaster Response Act
Short summary: Requires the President to develop a national strategy for using microreactors in natural disaster response. Involves consultation with multiple federal agencies and includes assessments, goals, and recommendations for deploying microreactors efficiently and safely, emphasizing technological integration and partnerships.

Official name: Forest Data Modernization Act of 2023
Casual name: Forest Data Modernization Act
Short summary: Amends the Forest and Rangeland Renewable Resources Research Act to integrate AI and advanced technologies, including machine learning, for enhanced forest data collection and analysis.
Long summary:
Amends the Forest and Rangeland Renewable Resources Research Act to include forest carbon in resource assessments.

Updates strategic plan to include consistent data collection protocols, integration of new technologies, and enhanced transparency.

Amends the Agriculture Improvement Act of 2018 to authorize microwave, LiDAR, hyperspectral, high-resolution remote sensing, and machine learning technologies for forest data analysis.
Tags: Strategies: Governance development

Official name: Know Your App Act
Casual name: Know Your App Act
Short summary: Requires application stores to prominently display the primary country of origin for each application. Allows users to filter or receive disclaimers for apps from concerning countries. Requires the Treasury and Commerce Secretaries to submit to Congress a list of foreign countries that subject app developers to control by the government of the country over content moderation, algorithm design, or user data transfers.
Long summary:
Requires covered companies to publicly list the primary country of origin of each application on the app store page with prominent display.

Mandates that covered companies provide an option to filter out apps from countries of concern and display disclaimers if data could be accessed by a foreign government.

Requires the Treasury and Commerce Secretaries to submit to Congress a list of foreign countries with a national law that subjects the app developers to control by the government of the country over content moderation, algorithm design, or user data transfers. Goes into effect 180 days after the act's enactment.
Tags: Strategies: Government study or report

Official name: National Mesonet Authorization Act
Casual name: National Mesonet Authorization Act
Short summary: Establishes the National Mesonet Program to improve weather observations using non-Federal data sources. Prioritizes collaboration with commercial, academic, and other sectors. Provides financial support for enhancing data collection capabilities. Mandates regular reporting and sets funding allocations through 2028.
Long summary:
Establishes the National Mesonet Program within the National Weather Service to improve weather observation and forecasting.

Requires the program to leverage commercial, academic, and other non-Federal weather data to enhance coordination across sectors.

Targets improved environmental observations to support baseline forecasts and warnings, and to enhance numerical weather prediction performance.

Mandates integration of greater density and types of weather observations, including local and regional networks and satellite constellations.

Provides financial and technical assistance for State, Tribal, private, and academic entities to build or upgrade mesonet systems, contingent on agreements to share data.

Forms an advisory committee to recommend improvements and expansions of available data.

Obligates regular briefings to relevant Congressional committees on program activities and progress.

Allocates specified funding amounts for fiscal years 2024 through 2028 to support the program's implementation and expansion.

Official name: Intelligence Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2024, Title V ("Matters Pertaining to United States Economic and Emerging Technology Competition with United States Adversaries"), Subtitle B ("Next Generation Energy, Biotechnology, and Artificial Intelligence")
Casual name: Intelligence Authorization Act FY 2024, Title V ("Emerging Technology Competition"), Subtitle B ("Next Generation AI")
Short summary: Amends intelligence assessments to include China's AI investments and establishes policies for AI capabilities within the intelligence community. Requires a strategy for notifying federal agencies about AI-related risks, including information security and biosecurity threats, by vendors and researchers.
Long summary:
**Subtitle B—Next-Generation Energy, Biotechnology, And Artificial Intelligence**

**SEC. 511. Expanded Annual Assessment of Economic and Technological Capabilities of the People's Republic of China.**

Requires a detailed assessment of China’s investments in AI, next-generation energy technologies, and biotechnology.

Identifies competitive practices and opportunities to counter them.

Determines U.S. vulnerabilities in the supply chain of these technologies.

**SEC. 514. Policies Established by Director of National Intelligence for Artificial Intelligence Capabilities.**

Mandates the establishment of policies for AI acquisition, adoption, development, use, coordination, and maintenance.

Requires guidelines for evaluating AI model performance, continuous monitoring, and documentation.

Calls for standards on imputed, augmented, or synthetic data used in AI training.

**SEC. 515. Strategy for Submittal of Notice by Private Persons to Federal Agencies Regarding Certain Risks and Threats Relating to Artificial Intelligence.**

Directs the President to establish a strategy for vendors and users to notify the government of AI-related risks.

Includes information security, biosecurity risks, foreign malign influence, and other unlawful activities related to AI.

Outlines a plan for Federal agencies to engage in public education on AI risks.

Official name: National Drone and Advanced Air Mobility Research and Development Act, Title II ("National Drone and Advanced Air Mobility Research Institutes")
Casual name: Drone R&D Act, Title II ("National Drone and Advanced Air Mobility Research Institutes")
Short summary: Establishes a program to fund Institutes focusing on unmanned aircraft systems, including AI research. Encourages partnerships, supports interdisciplinary education, and requires data management strategies. Allocates $5 million annually for 2024-2028. Prioritizes merit-based selection and avoids duplicative efforts.
Long summary:
Establishes a program to award financial assistance for creating and supporting a network of National Drone and Advanced Air Mobility Research Institutes.

Requires collaboration among public and private organizations, including Federal agencies, academic institutions, and industry, to form these Institutes.

Mandates the Institutes to focus on economic or social sectors and address ethical, societal, safety, and security implications of unmanned aircraft systems.

Supports interdisciplinary research, education, and workforce development, including broadening participation of underrepresented communities.

Provides funds for managing data sets, developing testbeds, conducting research, and supporting AI and machine learning related to advanced air mobility systems.

Imposes a competitive merit review process for awarding financial assistance and prohibits duplication of efforts.

Authorizes $5 million annually for fiscal years 2024-2028 to support these activities.

Official name: Intelligence Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2024, Sec. 909 ("Requirement to Ensure Intelligence Community Directives Appropriately Account for Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Tools in Intelligence Products")
Casual name: Intelligence Authorization Act FY 2024, Sec. 909 ("Ensure IC Directives Account for AI and ML Tools in Intelligence Products")
Short summary: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to brief Congress on AI and machine learning guidance in intelligence products within 120 days. Determines whether existing directives provide sufficient guidance and whether updates are necessary, including explanations or update plans and timelines.
Long summary:
Requires the Director of National Intelligence to brief congressional intelligence committees within 120 days on whether current intelligence community directives provide sufficient guidance on the use of AI and machine learning tools in intelligence products.

Mandates the briefing to include a determination of whether Intelligence Community Directives 203 and 206, and other relevant directives, offer adequate guidance on using, sourcing, and providing transparency for AI and machine learning tools.

Requires the Director to assess if these directives need updates to enhance guidance and direction.

Obligates the Director to explain why no updates are needed if deemed sufficient, or to provide a plan and timeline for necessary updates if improvements are required.
Tags: Strategies: Evaluation;Risk factors: Transparency

Official name: Intelligence Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2024, Sec. 906 ("Programs for Next-Generation Microelectronics in Support of Artificial Intelligence")
Casual name: Intelligence Authorization Act FY 2024, Sec. 906 ("Next-Generation Microelectronics for AI")
Short summary: Establishes a program to advance microelectronics research supporting AI that focuses on next-generation computing models and overcoming microelectronics challenges. Requires collaboration with government, academia, and industry, and mandates regular congressional briefings on progress.
Long summary:
Establishes a program to advance microelectronics research, overseen by the Director of National Intelligence through the Intelligence Advanced Research Projects Activity.

Focuses research on next-generation computing models, materials, devices, architectures, and algorithms to enhance artificial intelligence and machine learning.

Promotes advancements in computing technologies through multidisciplinary research, encompassing algorithm design, compute capability, generative AI, photonics-based microprocessors, new materials, optical networks, and AI safety for the intelligence community.

Requires active collaboration with government agencies, academic institutions, and private industry.

Authorizes appropriations for contracts, grants, and other transactions to support the program.

Mandates regular briefings to congressional intelligence committees on program progress, partnerships, and future research priorities.
Tags: Strategies: New institution;Strategies: Government support;Strategies: Government support: For R&D;Strategies: Convening

Official name: Weather Act Reauthorization Act of 2023, Sec. 502 ("National Integrated Drought Information System")
Casual name: Weather Act Reauthorization, Sec. 502 ("National Integrated Drought Information System")
Short summary: Amends the National Integrated Drought Information System to include AI, machine learning, and cloud technologies for drought monitoring and forecasting. Establishes partnerships for coordinated drought information and updates modeling to probabilistic forecasts.
Long summary:
Amends the National Integrated Drought Information System Act of 2006 to advance and deploy next-generation technologies related to drought, including artificial intelligence, machine learning, and cloud technologies.

Requires incorporation of flash drought research and tools to enhance timely response.

Mandates utilization of observational networks and refinement of drought indicators for decision-support products by optimizing data from across the Federal Government.

Establishes memoranda of understanding with the National Mesonet Program to provide coordinated, high-quality, nationwide drought information.

Directs the transition of existing drought products to probabilistic forecasts and incorporation of new dynamical and statistical forecast modeling tools.

Authorizes appropriations increasing annually from $15,000,000 in fiscal year 2024 to $17,000,000 in fiscal year 2028.
Tags: Strategies: Government support;Strategies: Government support: For R&D;Strategies: Convening;Strategies: Government support

Official name: Weather Act Reauthorization Act of 2023, Sec. 402 ("Hazardous Weather or Water Event Risk Communication")
Casual name: Weather Act Reauthorization, Sec. 402 ("Hazardous Weather Risk Communication")
Short summary: Improves NOAA's communication system for hazardous weather risks using social, behavioral, and AI research. Coordinates with governmental and educational partners to ensure timely, consistent public information dissemination.
Long summary:
Amends Section 406 of the Weather Research and Forecasting Innovation Act of 2017 to enhance communication about hazardous weather or water events.

Requires the Under Secretary to maintain and improve NOAA's system for communicating risks of hazardous weather or water events to the public.

Mandates the creation of a social, behavioral, risk, communication, and economic sciences program to simplify and improve communication of hazardous weather or water events.

Requires recommendations to be based on the latest social, behavioral, economic, risk, and communications science research, validated using rigorous methods, including AI and machine learning.

Ensures coordination with federal, state, local, tribal, educational, and media partners.

Establishes best practices for timely and consistent communications across public platforms.
Tags: Strategies: Convening

Official name: Weather Act Reauthorization Act of 2023, Sec. 304 ("Data Assimilation, Management, and Sharing Practices")
Casual name: Weather Act Reauthorization, Sec. 304 ("Data Assimilation, Management, and Sharing")
Short summary: Establishes data standards and infrastructure to support open science and efficient data transfer, including AI for data assimilation. Forms a consortium to advance data assimilation research and workforce development.
Long summary:
Establishes the requirement for the Under Secretary, in collaboration with the weather enterprise, to develop consistent and open data and metadata standards to support open science.

Instructs the establishment of a program to advance data assimilation methods, including AI and machine learning, and the creation of a university consortium to address research challenges in data assimilation.
Tags: Strategies: Evaluation;Strategies: Government support;Strategies: Government support: For R&D;Strategies: Government support: AI workforce-related;Strategies: Convening;Strategies: New institution

Official name: Weather Act Reauthorization Act of 2023, Sec. 116(f) ("National Harmful Algal Bloom and Hypoxia Observing Network")
Casual name: Weather Act Reauthorization, Sec. 116(f) ("Algal Bloom Observing Network")
Short summary: Integrates Federal, State, regional, and local capabilities to establish a national network for harmful algal bloom monitoring, incorporating artificial intelligence. Establishes a Harmful Algal Bloom Data Assembly Center for data integration and ecological forecasting support.
Long summary:
Amends Section 606 of the Harmful Algal Bloom and Hypoxia Research and Control Act to establish a national harmful algal bloom observing network.

Requires the Under Secretary, via NCCOS and IOOS, to integrate Federal, State, regional, and local observing capabilities.

Mandates the use of emerging technologies and data integration methods, including artificial intelligence, for monitoring, detecting, and forecasting harmful algal blooms.

Instructs the IOOS Program Office to coordinate with NCCOS for observations, data integration, and information dissemination.

Establishes a Harmful Algal Bloom Data Assembly Center to integrate, disseminate, and support ecological forecasting.
Tags: Strategies: New institution;Strategies: Convening;Applications: Medicine, life sciences and public health;Strategies: New institution;Strategies: Convening

Official name: Weather Act Reauthorization Act of 2023, Sec. 108 ("Computing Resources Prioritization")
Casual name: Weather Act Reauthorization, Sec. 108 ("Computing Resources Prioritization")
Short summary: Requires the Under Secretary and Secretary of Energy to leverage AI and machine learning for NOAA's missions. Establishes centers of excellence for AI advancements. Mandates a report evaluating initiative effectiveness and needs for computing collaboration with the Department of Energy.
Long summary:
Amends the Weather Research and Forecasting Innovation Act to mandate collaboration between the Under Secretary and the Secretary of Energy in leveraging high-performance computing and AI for advanced weather forecasting.

Requires a competitive, merit-reviewed process for initiative applications, including Federal agencies, National Laboratories, and higher education institutions.

Mandates coordination of AI and computing research across NOAA to avoid duplication and enhance predictive analysis.

Supports model comparison, data sharing, interagency collaboration, long-term data archiving, agile computing decisions, workforce development, and compliance with federal research security requirements. 

Directs sharing of model innovations through community-based activities and interagency coordination.

Authorizes leveraging AI to enhance NOAA’s computing infrastructure for critical missions.

Allows establishment of centers of excellence for AI and machine learning in weather forecasting.

Permits multi-year contracts for high-performance and cloud computing systems.

Requires a report to Congress evaluating the initiative’s effectiveness and future computing needs within two years.
Tags: Strategies: Government support;Strategies: Government support: For R&D;Strategies: Government support: AI workforce-related;Strategies: Convening;Applications: Government: other applications/unspecified;Strategies: New institution;Strategies: Government support;Strategies: Government support: AI workforce-related;Strategies: Convening;Strategies: Government support: For R&D;Strategies: Government support;Strategies: Government support: For R&D;Strategies: Government study or report

Official name: Research and Development Program on New Approaches to Data Analysis for Aviation Safety
Casual name: Research and Development Program on New Approaches to Data Analysis for Aviation Safety
Short summary: Establishes a research program using AI and machine learning for aviation safety data analysis. Requires collaboration among FAA, NEXTOR III, NASA, and industry partners. Prioritizes understanding human factors and improving automated aircraft design. Authorizes $20 million annually through 2029.
Long summary:
Establishes a new research and development program by the FAA’s NEXTOR III to develop new data analysis approaches for aviation safety.

Includes use of new algorithms and AI methods for analyzing various data sets such as weather, equipment performance, flight delays, safety incidents, and communications.

Requires collaboration among Consortium member institutions, aviation industry partners, FAA units, and NASA’s Aviation Safety Reporting System for data sharing and testing.

Prioritizes understanding human factors contributing to aviation safety risk, including fatigue, distraction, workload, organizational culture, communication, and adherence to safety procedures.

Seeks to improve the design of highly automated aircraft to reduce mode confusion and increase awareness of flight parameters.

Authorizes $20,000,000 annually from 2024 to 2029 for program execution, including grants to participating institutions and FAA units.

Terminates the program 6 years after establishment.

Official name: Counseling for Career Choice Act
Casual name: Counseling for Career Choice Act
Short summary: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act to enhance career counseling, including using AI for career development.
Long summary:
Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to expand career counseling opportunities.

Requires career counseling programs to leverage resources and emerging technologies, including artificial intelligence.

Official name: New York A7106
Casual name: New York A7106 ("PAID Act")
Short summary: Amends election law to require disclosure of synthetic media in use in printed or digital political communications by writing or speaking, "This political communication was created with the assistance of artificial intelligence." Defines synthetic media as AI-modified text, image, video, or sound.
Long summary:
Amends the election law to mandate disclosure of synthetic media in political communications.

Requires disclosure for printed or digital political communications to display, "This political communication was created with the assistance of artificial intelligence."

Mandates non-printed or digital political communications, such as radio, to clearly display or speak the same statement.

Defines "synthetic media" as any form of media, including text, image, video, or sound, created or modified using AI algorithms.

Sets the effective date of this act to January 1, 2024.
Tags: Strategies: Disclosure;Strategies: Disclosure: In deployment

Official name: Deceptive Experiences To Online Users Reduction Act
Casual name: DETOUR Act
Short summary: Prohibits large online operators from designing user interfaces that impair user autonomy or encourage compulsive usage. Requires affirmative express consent for certain research and mandates disclosure of such research. Establishes independent review boards and empowers the FTC to enforce compliance.
Long summary:
Prohibits large online operators from manipulating user interfaces to obscure, subvert, or impair user autonomy, decision making, or consent.

Requires affirmative express consent from users for covered research and prohibits manipulation aimed at children or teens to encourage compulsive usage.

Mandates large online operators to disclose the purpose of covered research to users every 90 days and ensure transparency.

Obligates large online operators to establish independent review boards to oversee user interface design and research, ensuring compliance and preventing exploitative practices.

Directs the National Institute of Standards and Technology to develop resources for user interface design promoting user autonomy, choice, and decision making.

Empowers the Federal Trade Commission to enforce compliance, establish regulations for independent review boards, and define acceptable conduct for large online operators.

Provides safe harbor for operators acting in good faith per established guidelines.

Official name: National Drone and Advanced Air Mobility Research and Development Act, Title VI ("Department of Energy Activities")
Casual name: Drone R&D Act, Title VI ("DOE Activities")
Short summary: Advances unmanned aircraft systems and AI technologies through research, education, and financial support. Establishes data management strategies and interagency coordination. Allocates funding from fiscal years 2024 to 2028 for eligible entities to enhance DOE missions.
Long summary:
Mandates the Secretary of Energy to execute a cross-cutting research, development, and demonstration program for unmanned aircraft system (UAS) technologies, including artificial intelligence.

Requires coordination across relevant DOE offices, including the Artificial Intelligence Technology Office.

Requires the Secretary to set goals for unmanned aircraft systems research; build on existing research efforts; provide undergraduate and graduate training across AI, machine learning, sensing, materials, and energy technologies; establish a robust, interoperable data management strategy; and support dedicated institutes to advance unmanned aircraft systems and DOE mission priorities.

Awards financial assistance for research in both fundamental S&T areas and applications.

Mandates the Secretary to promote technology transfer by partnering with industry,
make available DOE research facilities and high-performance computing infrastructure, and coordinate with other federal departments to avoid duplication and ensure complementary activities.

Authorizes appropriations totaling $276,282,638 from fiscal years 2024 to 2028.
Tags: Strategies: Government support;Strategies: Government support: For R&D;Strategies: Convening;Strategies: Government support;Strategies: Government support: For R&D;Strategies: Government support: AI workforce-related;Strategies: Government support;Strategies: Government support: For R&D;Strategies: Convening;Strategies: Government support;Strategies: Pilots and testbeds

Official name: National Drone and Advanced Air Mobility Research and Development Act, Title IV ("National Science Foundation Activities")
Casual name: Drone R&D Act, Title IV ("NSF Activities")
Short summary: Supports research and education on AI integration into advanced air mobility and unmanned aircraft, encouraging ethical use, cybersecurity, data management, and workforce development. Establishes partnerships and coordinates with agencies to enhance domestic development, allocating specific funds through 2028.
Long summary:
Mandates the National Science Foundation (NSF) to support research and STEM education in advanced air mobility and unmanned aircraft systems.

Supports fundamental research on technologies including AI, safety, autonomous control, materials, and communications systems.

Funds research on practical applications such as weather prediction, disaster response, and infrastructure monitoring.

Supports research on data modeling, validation, security, and ethical use, including privacy concerns.

Encourages middle and high school STEM education related to unmanned aircraft systems through curriculum development and student engagement activities.

Promotes undergraduate and graduate education through curriculum development, hands-on research opportunities, and scholarships.

Develops a skilled technical workforce, including veterans, through national centers and community college programs.

Facilitates public-private partnerships and interagency coordination to avoid research duplication.

Authorizes appropriations increasing from $50 million in 2024 to nearly $61 million in 2028.
Tags: Strategies: Government support;Strategies: Government support: For R&D;Strategies: Government support;Strategies: Government support: For R&D;Strategies: Government support: AI workforce-related;Risk factors: Reliability;Risk factors: Security;Risk factors: Security: Cybersecurity;Risk factors: Privacy;Applications: Agriculture and resource extraction;Applications: Education;Strategies: Convening;Strategies: Government support

Official name: Food and Agriculture Industry Cybersecurity Support Act
Casual name: Food and Agriculture Industry Cybersecurity Support Act
Short summary: Establishes a cybersecurity clearinghouse for the food and agriculture industry. Requires consolidation of cybersecurity recommendations and consultations with various stakeholders. Mandates a study on federal efforts to improve industry cybersecurity.
Long summary:
Defines key terms for NTIA Food and Agriculture Cybersecurity Clearinghouse, including "cybersecurity risk" and "food and agriculture industry."

Establishes a food and agriculture cybersecurity clearinghouse within the NTIA to provide publicly available online resources focused on cybersecurity for the food and agriculture industry.

Requires the clearinghouse to be publicly available online and provide current cybersecurity resources focused on the food and agriculture industry, a support request mechanism, regularly updated FAQ section, and targeted materials for small and non-technical users on critical cybersecurity protections.

Requires the Assistant Secretary to work with relevant agencies to consolidate public and private sector best practices into a set of voluntary cybersecurity recommendations for the food and agriculture industry, including risk-based and cybersecurity-informed engineering, system control planning, protection measures, threat defenses, ransomware response, and data integrity recommendations.

Instructs the Assistant Secretary to consult with private sector, federal agencies, trade groups, and civil society organizations in implementing the clearinghouse.

Directs the Comptroller General to study and report on federal efforts to improve cybersecurity in the food and agriculture industry, evaluating effectiveness, resources, coordination, and potential risks of coordinated attacks.

Sunsets the section seven years after enactment.
Tags: Strategies: New institution;Strategies: Disclosure;Risk factors: Security;Risk factors: Security: Cybersecurity;Strategies: Convening;Risk factors: Security;Risk factors: Security: Cybersecurity;Risk factors: Reliability: Robustness;Risk factors: Reliability;Strategies: Convening;Strategies: Government study or report;Strategies: Government study or report;Strategies: Convening;Strategies: Convening

Official name: Technology Workforce Framework Act of 2024
Casual name: S. 3792 Technology Workforce Framework Act
Short summary: Requires the Director of the National Institute of Standards and Technology to develop and update an AI workforce framework. Expands NIST's functions to include workforce frameworks for emerging technologies. Mandates reports to Congress on frameworks and their applications.
Long summary:
Expands the functions of the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) to include developing workforce frameworks for critical and emerging technologies, including artificial intelligence (AI).

Requires the Director of NIST to develop, maintain, and update an AI workforce framework and review it at least every three years.

Mandates the inclusion of professional, support, and operational skills in AI workforce frameworks, considering nontraditional backgrounds.

Instructs the Director to consult with various stakeholders, including federal agencies and educational institutions, in developing these frameworks.

Obliges the Director to produce multilingual resources to support global adoption of these frameworks.

Requires periodic reports to Congress on updates and the process of reviewing workforce frameworks, including the National Initiative for Cybersecurity Education Workforce Framework for Cybersecurity.

Directs the development and publication of additional workforce frameworks for other critical technologies, such as quantum information science.

Official name: Preventing Adversaries from Developing Critical Capabilities Act
Casual name: Preventing Adversaries from Developing Critical Capabilities Act
Short summary: Defines “covered sectors” to include artificial intelligence and directs the President to identify such technologies posing national security risks when developed or acquired by countries of concern. Requires regulations to prohibit certain covered activities, mandate notification for others, and authorizes enforcement, reporting, and penalties to implement these restrictions.
Long summary:
Prohibits U.S. persons from engaging in covered activities involving certain technologies and products in countries of concern, particularly in sectors like AI, if they pose acute national security threats.

Requires the President to annually identify and publish lists of such critical technologies and products.

Mandates U.S. persons to notify the President of any covered activities that may threaten national security.

Empowers the President to impose civil penalties for violations and to delegate enforcement authority to federal agencies.

Encourages bilateral and multilateral engagements to promote compliance with these prohibitions and notifications.

Allocates $25 million annually for implementation and allows direct hiring to support administration.

Permits the President to issue waivers for activities deemed in the national interest, with required congressional notification.

Official name: North Carolina SB 460
Casual name: North Carolina SB 460 ("Automation and Workforce")
Short summary: Establishes a Study Committee on Automation and the Workforce to assess the impact of automation technologies, including AI, on North Carolina's workforce, especially low-income and minority workers. Requires biennial reports with recommendations to the Governor and legislature.
Long summary:
Establishes the Study Committee on Automation and the Workforce to analyze the impact of automation on North Carolina's workforce, with a focus on low-income and minority workers.

Requires the Committee to review the current and future impact of artificial intelligence, industrial robotics, self-service systems and productivity software on the State's low-income workforce.

Requires the Committee to identify the education, training, retraining, and skill-development needed to help low-income workers adapt to automation and reduce job displacement

Requires the Committee to evaluate how the State and employers can work together to sustain and create employment opportunities in response to the current and future impacts of automation.

Requires the Committee to report its findings and recommendations to the Governor and Joint Legislative Commission on Governmental Operations before the 2025 General Assembly and biennially thereafter.
Tags: Strategies: New institution;Strategies: Convening;Strategies: Evaluation;Strategies: Government study or report

Official name: American Privacy Rights Act of 2024, Sec. 114-5
Casual name: American Privacy Rights Act of 2024, Sec. 114-5
Short summary: Establishes a pilot program to promote privacy-enhancing technologies like homomorphic encryption and federated learning. Requires development of a petition process and an auditing system for oversight. Mandates ongoing audits and allows withdrawal for non-compliance. Provides liability limitations for compliant participants.
Long summary:
Establishes a pilot program to encourage private sector use of privacy-enhancing technologies within one year of the Act's enactment to protect covered data.

Requires the Commission to develop a petition process for entities to join the pilot program and implement an auditing system using privacy-enhancing technologies for enforcement purposes.

Mandates ongoing audits of participating entities to ensure compliance with privacy-enhancing technologies.

Allows covered entities deemed compliant to be considered in compliance with section 109, with a rebuttable presumption in certain legal actions.

Obligates the Comptroller General to study the pilot program's progress and effectiveness, with an initial briefing and a final report to Congress.

Creates a new bureau within the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to enforce the title with experts, including technologists experienced with AI.

Establishes a Privacy and Security Victims Relief Fund for consumer redress and technological research.

Requires the FTC to report to Congress on investigations and future plans related to the title.

Official name: Comment Integrity and Management Act of 2024
Casual name: Comment Integrity and Management Act
Short summary: Amends the E-Government Act to require verification of human origin for electronic comments, identify mass and AI-generated comments, and allow agencies to post representative samples. Directs OMB to issue guidance for managing these comments and mandates policy updates.
Long summary:
Amends the E-Government Act of 2002 to improve the handling of mass and computer-generated comments in the regulatory review process.

Requires agencies to verify whether electronic comments are submitted by humans.

Mandates agencies to identify and label mass comments and allows agencies to post representative samples of these comments.

Directs the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to issue guidance on verifying human submissions and managing mass comments, including the use of technology tools.

Obliges agencies to establish and publicly post policies on handling computer-generated and mass comments within one year of enactment.

Compels the Government Accountability Office (GAO) to report on the identification and impact of computer-generated comments within two years.
Tags: Strategies: Government study or report

Official name: Strategy for Public Health Preparedness and Response to Artificial Intelligence Threats
Casual name: S. 2346 (Public Health Response to AI)
Short summary: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to develop and report to Congress a strategy addressing AI misuse threats to national health security. Involves public health preparedness, identifying gaps, and mitigating risks like biological weapons.
Long summary:
Mandates the Secretary of Health and Human Services to develop a strategy for public health preparedness and response to artificial intelligence (AI) threats within 180 days of enactment with the following requirements: a framework for public health preparedness and response to mitigate the risks of misuse of artificial intelligence; an outline of the duties, functions, and preparedness goals for which the Secretary is responsible; gaps in public health capabilities and strategies to address these gaps and strengthen emergency preparedness against AI threats; strategies to mitigate risks, such as the development of biological weapons and treatment-resistant viruses and bacteria.

Requires the strategy to be submitted to relevant congressional committees without compromising national security.

Amends the Public Health Service Act to integrate this AI threat strategy into the broader federal response to public health emergencies.
Tags: Strategies: Government study or report;Risk factors: Safety;Strategies: Convening

Official name: National Wildland Fire Risk Reduction Program Act, Sec. 7 (Responsibilities of Program Agencies)
Casual name: National Wildland Fire Risk Reduction Program Act, Sec. 7 (Responsibilities of Program Agencies)
Short summary: Assigns responsibilities to various U.S. agencies for wildland fire research, prediction, and mitigation, including AI-driven data analysis and modeling. Focuses on developing tools, improving fire-weather forecasts, enhancing coordination, and promoting fire-resistant infrastructure and practices.
Long summary:
Mandates NIST to conduct research on wildland fire impacts, firebrands, novel materials, and performance-based mitigation tools.

Requires NIST to coordinate federal wildland-urban interface fire investigations and develop methodologies for assessing fire impacts on communities.

Directs NSF to support research on wildland fire risks, tools, technologies, and the social and economic factors influencing fire risk reduction.

Instructs NOAA to improve wildland fire understanding through research, weather forecasting, data interoperability, and high-performance computing.

Tasks NOAA with establishing a Fire Weather Testbed and supporting extramural research on wildland fires.

Requires FEMA to support community risk assessment tools, public outreach, and best practices for fire-resistant construction and response.

Directs NASA to support wildland fire research, modeling, data systems, and the development of novel monitoring tools.

Instructs EPA to research wildland fire smoke characteristics, impacts on air and water quality, and communication of risk reduction strategies.

Mandates DOE to develop tools for energy sector resilience to wildland fires and coordinate data resources for improved fire understanding and prevention.

Official name: Online Privacy Act of 2023, Sec. 106 ("Right to individual autonomy")
Casual name: Online Privacy Act, Sec. 106 ("Individual autonomy")
Short summary: Prohibits collecting, processing, maintaining, or disclosing personal information for behavioral personalization without consent. Requires annual consent renewals. Mandates non-personalized versions if consent is denied. Allows service denial if non-personalization is infeasible. Exempts small businesses.
Long summary:
Prohibits covered entities from collecting, processing, maintaining, or disclosing personal information for behavioral personalization without affirmative express consent.

Requires covered entities to obtain express affirmative consent before providing behaviorally personalized products or services, and annually thereafter.

Mandates providing non-personalized versions of products or services if consent is denied, or core aspects if full functionality without personalization is infeasible.

Allows denial of products or services if no core aspect can function without personalization and consent is not given.

Permits processing personal information for usability improvements using aggregated data, provided the output is uniform and independent of individual characteristics.

Excludes optimizations aimed primarily at increasing user engagement time from the definition of "usability."

Exempts small businesses from these requirements.

Official name: 21st Century Peace through Strength Act, Division D ("Protecting Americans from Foreign Adversary Controlled Applications Act")
Casual name: 21st Century Peace through Strength Act, Division D ("Foreign Adversary Controlled Applications")
Short summary: Prohibits entities from distributing, maintaining, or updating foreign adversary-controlled applications in the U.S. Requires user data portability before prohibition. Allows civil penalties for violations. Permits exemptions for qualified divestitures and necessary services. Grants judicial review in D.C. Circuit.
Long summary:
Prohibits entities from distributing, maintaining, or updating foreign adversary controlled applications within the U.S.

Requires data portability for users before prohibitions apply, enabling users to access their data in a machine-readable format.

Exempts applications undergoing "qualified divestiture" from prohibitions.

Enforces penalties up to $5,000 per user for distribution violations and $500 per user for data portability violations.

Mandates the Attorney General to investigate and enforce compliance through civil penalties or court actions.

Defines "foreign adversary controlled applications" and "qualified divestiture" to ensure clarity in application and exemptions.

Grants judicial review exclusively to the U.S. Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit, with specified time limits for challenges.

Official name: Water Infrastructure Modernization Act of 2023
Casual name: Water Infrastructure Modernization Act
Short summary: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act and Safe Drinking Water Act to authorize grants for smart wastewater and smart water infrastructure technology. Supports AI and intelligent optimization tools to improve water systems' efficiency, reliability, and decision-making.
Long summary:
Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act and the Safe Drinking Water Act to authorize grants for smart wastewater and smart water infrastructure technology.

Requires the Administrator to provide grants for intelligent wastewater treatment systems using AI for predictive maintenance and real-time monitoring.

Mandates grants for smart drinking water systems with AI tools to reduce water loss, improve decision-making, and enhance operational efficiency.

Requires annual reports to Congress on the projects funded and their impacts.
Tags: Strategies: Government support;Strategies: Government study or report;Strategies: Government support;Strategies: Government study or report

Official name: Advanced Weather Model Computing Development Act
Casual name: S. 3642 Weather Model Computing Act
Short summary: Directs the Secretary of Energy and NOAA Administrator to conduct collaborative research using AI and advanced computing to improve weather models. Establishes initiatives and potential centers of excellence, promoting data sharing and workforce development.
Long summary:
Defines key terms regarding the use of AI in developing weather models.

Directs the Secretary of Energy and the NOAA Administrator to conduct collaborative research using advanced computing techniques, including AI, to improve weather models.

Requires establishment of a memorandum of understanding or similar agreement to facilitate this collaboration through a competitive, merit-reviewed process.

Supports the Secretary and Administrator to explore performance portability for weather models, advance large-scale data analysis methods, promote open collaboration and data sharing, support computing infrastructure, and use reimbursable agreements and interagency collaboration to maximize effectiveness.

Initiates a computing research initiative to develop and deploy advanced weather models using advanced computing techniques, including coordinating research to prevent duplication, supporting workforce development and infrastructure, conducting comparative forecast research, and sharing model innovations.

Permits establishment of up to three centers of excellence at National Laboratories to support advanced weather models through public-private partnerships, software optimization, and workforce development; prioritizes selection of Laboratories with AI-enabled computing systems and expertise in weather and climate modeling.

Mandates submission of reports to Congress detailing interagency coordination, achievements, and future opportunities for collaboration in advanced computing techniques.

Official name: HEARTS Act of 2022
Casual name: HEARTS Act
Short summary: Prioritizes nonanimal methods in NIH-supported research proposals; establishes the National Center for Alternatives to Animals in Research and Testing to promote nonanimal research methods, including AI.
Long summary:
Prioritizes nonanimal methods in NIH-supported research proposals.

Establishes the National Center for Alternatives to Animals in Research and Testing within NIH to develop, promote, and fund nonanimal research methods, including AI.

Mandates the center to provide training and resources to researchers on nonanimal methods.
Tags: Strategies: New institution;Strategies: Government support: For R&D;Strategies: Government support: AI workforce-related;Strategies: Convening;Strategies: Government support;Applications: Medicine, life sciences and public health

Official name: Department of Defense Financial Audits Using Artificial Intelligence Act
Casual name: DoD Financial Audits Using AI Act
Short summary: Directs the Secretary of Defense to use AI technology for auditing the Department of Defense's 2024 financial statements. Requires AI development to improve audits and replace outdated systems. Mandates surplus funds' rescission and briefing on audit progress and AI technology.
Long summary:
Directs the Secretary of Defense to ensure the fiscal year 2024 audit of the Department of Defense's financial statements uses AI technology.

Requires the Under Secretary of Defense (Comptroller) and the Inspector General to jointly develop the necessary AI technology for facilitating these audits.

Mandates the use of this AI technology to accelerate the retirement of older financial management systems without compromising essential controls or auditing standards.

Orders the rescission and deposit of any identified surplus funds from the audit into the General Fund of the Treasury for deficit reduction.

Obliges the Secretary of Defense to brief the Senate and House Committees on Armed Services within 180 days on the audit's progress and the AI technology development.

Official name: Council of Europe Framework Convention on Artificial Intelligence and Human Rights, Democracy and the Rule of Law
Casual name: Council of Europe Framework Convention on AI
Short summary: Ensures AI activities align with human rights, democracy, and the rule of law and requires measures for transparency, accountability, non-discrimination, and privacy protection. Establishes oversight guidelines and risk management frameworks for signatories and encourages cooperation between signatories and with other states on AI-related issues.
Long summary:
Mandates signatories to adopt measures ensuring AI systems align with human rights, democracy, and the rule of law.

Explains that signatories are not required to apply the Convention to activities within the lifecycle of AI related to the protection of national security interests, but insists that such activities adhere to international human rights law and respect democratic processes.

Requires implementation of measures that ensure transparency, accountability, and non-discrimination, and the protection of privacy, human dignity, and democratic processes within the lifecycle of AI systems.

Calls for the adoption of remedies and procedural safeguards for human rights violations resulting from activities within the lifecycle of AI systems.

Demands the creation of risk assessment and mitigation strategies for AI, with a focus on risks to human rights, democracy, and the rule of law.

Encourages signatories to consult the public on AI–related questions and promote digital literacy and skills to the entire population.

Establishes a follow-up mechanism for the implementation of the Convention and encourages signatories to cooperate with relevant stakeholders and other states on AI-related challenges.

Stipulates that signatories must establish or designate independent oversight mechanisms to ensure compliance with the Convention.

Permits dispute settlements, amendments to, and denunciations of the Convention.

Official name: National Drone and Advanced Air Mobility Research and Development Act, Title VII ("Department of Homeland Security Activities")
Casual name: Drone R&D Act, Title VII ("DHS Activities")
Short summary: Establishes research support for advanced air mobility and unmanned aircraft systems, emphasizing cybersecurity, safety, and data management. Creates a Counter-UAS Center of Excellence. Allocates funding for research, development, and coordination with federal agencies to prevent duplicative efforts.
Long summary:
Mandates the Secretary of Homeland Security to support research, development, evaluation, and testing of advanced air mobility, unmanned aircraft systems (UAS), and counter-UAS systems.

Directs coordination with relevant Department of Homeland Security offices and programs, including the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency and U.S. Customs and Border Protection.

Requires production of standardized, secure, and privacy-protected data sets for advanced air mobility and UAS systems.

Supports establishing one or more Institutes to advance research in these fields.

Establishes a Counter-UAS Center of Excellence within the Department of Homeland Security.

Specifies selection criteria for host institutions, emphasizing past performance, geographic proximity to federal facilities, and capabilities in AI and machine learning.

Permits use of funds for fundamental research, evaluation, education, and training related to counter-UAS systems.

Sets the federal share of grants at no more than 75% of costs.

Authorizes appropriations totaling $30 million to $36.5 million from fiscal years 2024 to 2028.

Official name: New Generation Artificial Intelligence Development Plan
Casual name: New Generation Artificial Intelligence Development Plan
Short summary: Aims to establish China as an AI leader by 2030. Promotes AI integration, key technology development, and military-civilian integration. Sets strategic objectives, standards, and regulations. Supports talent cultivation, international cooperation, and governance structures for implementation and evaluation.
Long summary:
The "New Generation Artificial Intelligence Development Plan" aims to establish China as a global leader in AI by 2030.

Promotes AI integration into economic and social sectors, enhancing national competitiveness and security.

Mandates the development of AI key technologies, including autonomous systems, knowledge computing, and intelligent robots.

Sets strategic objectives to achieve global AI leadership by 2020, 2025, and 2030, with specific economic targets and regulatory frameworks.

Emphasizes building AI innovation systems, fostering high-end AI industries, and upgrading industrial and social intelligentization.

Encourages military-civilian integration of AI technologies and development of AI-related infrastructure.

Establishes standards, regulations, and ethical guidelines to ensure safe, reliable AI development.

Supports talent cultivation and international cooperation to enhance AI R&D and application capabilities.

Outlines governance structures for implementing AI strategies and plans, including task monitoring and evaluation mechanisms.

Official name: Fraudulent Artificial Intelligence Regulations (FAIR) Elections Act of 2024
Casual name: Fraudulent Artificial Intelligence Regulations (FAIR) Elections Act of 2024
Short summary: Prohibits knowingly distributing false AI-generated election media to mislead voters or intimidate officials. Exempts certain media organizations if falsehoods are clearly disclosed. Authorizes the Attorney General to enforce violations.
Long summary:
Prohibits the distribution of false AI-generated election media containing materially false information about election details or voter eligibility with the intent to impede voting in federal elections.

Prohibits the distribution of false AI-generated depictions of election officials intended to intimidate or harass officials or deter voting.

Exempts bona fide news entities from these prohibitions if they clearly disclose the falsity of the AI-generated content.

Empowers the Attorney General to enforce these prohibitions through civil actions in U.S. District Courts.

Amends the National Voter Registration Act to prevent states from using unverified databases to remove voters from official lists, requiring government-verified information approved by the Attorney General.

Sets criteria for source information, including monthly updates and non-violation of the Computer Fraud and Abuse Act.

Ensures severability of the Act’s provisions, maintaining the validity of remaining sections if any part is invalidated.
Tags: Incentives: Civil liability;Harms: Detrimental content

Official name: AREA Act, Part B ("National Center for Education Research"), Sec. 133 ("Duties")
Casual name: AREA Act, Part B ("National Center for Education Research"), Sec. 133 ("Duties")
Short summary: Amends duties of the Research Commissioner to support AI and digital literacy research, coordinate evidence use, and advance educational technology. Mandates AI-focused studies on educational technology, including developing evidence-based practices and supporting research-practice partnerships with AI considerations.
Long summary:
Amends the duties of the Research Commissioner to include maintaining peer-review standards and supporting scientifically valid research.

Requires the Commissioner to propose and implement a research plan, ensuring the research is relevant and informs decision-making.

Mandates the support of evidence-based practices and the dissemination of research findings.

Grants authority to conduct research on underrepresented student populations and to support research-practice partnerships.

Obligates the Commissioner to ensure research is reviewed for inclusion in the National Education Research Database.

Allows for the provision of technical assistance to underrepresented researchers and institutions.

Supports research on educational technology, including artificial intelligence, digital literacy, and other significant educational topics.

Official name: Tech to Save Moms Act, Sec. 5 ("Report on the Use of Technology in Maternity Care")
Casual name: Tech to Save Moms Act, Sec. 5 ("Report on Technology in Maternity Care")
Short summary: Requires the National Academies to study AI's role in maternal health care, examine biases, and devise best practices for bias reduction, privacy, and security. Mandates reporting to Congress within 24 months of enactment.
Long summary:
Mandates the Secretary of Health and Human Services to arrange a study with the National Academies on technology use in maternity care.

Requires examination of innovative technology, including AI, and its impact on racial or ethnic biases in maternal health.

Demands analysis of patient monitoring devices and their influence on racial or ethnic biases.

Calls for identification of best practices to reduce and prevent racial or ethnic biases in technology and device use.

Seeks best practices for using these technologies specifically for racial and ethnic minority groups in maternity care.

Instructs evaluation of privacy and security safeguards related to these technologies.

Obliges the National Academies to complete the study and report results to Congress within 24 months of enactment.
Tags: Strategies: Convening;Strategies: Government study or report;Harms: Discrimination;Risk factors: Safety;Harms: Harm to health/safety;Strategies: Government study or report

Official name: LISTOS Act
Casual name: LISTOS Act
Short summary: Requires large online platforms to ensure consistent enforcement of content moderation, including through automated processes, across languages. Mandates disclosures on processes, establishes an advisory group, authorizes enforcement by the Federal Trade Commission or State Attorneys General, and establishes a pilot program for international research, including tool development, to address non-English online communication inciting harm.
Long summary:
Requires covered platform operators to ensure that content moderation processes are reasonably consistent across languages in which the platform conducts monetization practices, while allowing enforcing entities to consider practical limitations and prohibiting mandates on specific content decisions.

Requires platform operators to report on processes, including automated processes, for content moderation annually to the Federal Trade Commission (Commission) and the public.

Requires platform operators to ensure that user tools for reporting content and automated actions are accessible in all languages offered by the platform.

Establishes the Advisory Group on Language-Sensitive Technologies under the Commission to provide best practices on the application of language-sensitive AI technologies to ensure nondiscriminatory application, and for the Commission to make available the results.

Empowers the Commission and State Attorneys General to enforce compliance, treating violations as unfair or deceptive practices under the Federal Trade Commission Act.

Mandates the Commission to promulgate further regulations for carrying out the Act within 120 days.

Directs the United States Agency for International Development, in coordination with the Department of State, to set up a pilot program supporting international research, including tool development, to address non-English online communication that incites harm, and to report results to Congress and make them publicly available. Authorizes $3,000,000 in each of the fiscal years 2024 and 2025 in appropriations for the program.

Official name: RESTRICT Act
Casual name: RESTRICT Act
Short summary: Authorizes the Secretary of Commerce to identify, prohibit, and mitigate risks from foreign adversary transactions and holdings involving AI, ICT, and other critical technologies, and allows the President to order divestment when such investments threaten national security.
Long summary:
Authorizes the Secretary of Commerce to review, restrict, or prohibit certain information and communications technology (ICT) transactions between U.S. persons and foreign adversaries when they pose national security risks.

Empowers the Secretary to identify, deter, disrupt, investigate, or mitigate risks from covered transactions, which are broadly defined to include dealings with foreign adversaries or entities subject to their jurisdiction. 

Defines "covered transactions" broadly, including those involving foreign adversaries or entities under their jurisdiction.

Establishes procedures for the Secretary to refer covered holdings posing national security risks to the President, who can then compel divestment or take other actions to mitigate risks.

Prioritizes critical ICT areas such as AI, quantum computing, and telecommunications for evaluation.

Exempts actions from standard administrative and judicial reviews, limiting legal challenges to specific high courts.

Imposes civil and criminal penalties for violations, including fines, imprisonment, and forfeiture.

Official name: Weather Information for Agriculture Act
Casual name: Weather Information for Agriculture Act
Short summary: Advances the use of AI and machine learning for drought forecasting, strengthens federal drought and soil-moisture monitoring networks, and establishes partnerships for nationwide drought information. Optimizes federal data for drought indicators and authorizes funding for technology upgrades and national soil moisture monitoring.
Long summary:
Reauthorizes the National Integrated Drought Information System and expands its responsibilities.

Requires advancement and deployment of AI, machine learning, cloud technologies, and updated modeling tools for drought forecasting, including a shift to probabilistic forecasts.

Mandates strengthening observational networks and drought indicators by optimizing federal data and addressing gaps in snowpack, soil moisture, groundwater, and rapid-intensification monitoring.

Establishes memoranda of understanding with the National Mesonet Program for coordinated drought information.

Requires migration to probabilistic forecasts and incorporation of new modeling tools with the Climate Prediction Center.

Authorizes appropriations from $15,000,000 in fiscal year 2024 to $17,000,000 in fiscal year 2028 for these activities.

Mandates the development, deployment, and maintenance of the National Coordinated Soil Moisture Monitoring Network.

Requires the Under Secretary to develop, deploy, and maintain the National Coordinated Soil Moisture Monitoring Network, including establishing a user-friendly website, setting criteria for high-quality data, expanding in situ monitoring stations, improving soil-moisture products, and supporting research.

Promotes research, data sharing, and engagement with the citizen science community for soil moisture monitoring, allowing partnerships to provide nationwide soil moisture information.
Tags: Strategies: Convening;Applications: Agriculture and resource extraction;Strategies: Governance development;Strategies: Evaluation;Strategies: Government support;Strategies: Government support: For R&D;Applications: Agriculture and resource extraction;Strategies: Government support;Strategies: Government support: For R&D;Applications: Agriculture and resource extraction

Official name: Data Science and Literacy Act of 2023
Casual name: Data Science and Literacy Act
Short summary: Directs the Secretary of Education to award competitive grants to enhance data literacy education, including areas such as data science, statistics, and AI, from pre-kindergarten through postsecondary levels. Supports curriculum development, educator training, student support, and partnerships with industry and educational institutions, with reporting and evaluation requirements. Authorizes $10 million annually from 2024 to 2028.
Long summary:
Directs the Secretary of Education to award competitive grants to eligible entities to enhance data literacy education from pre-kindergarten through postsecondary levels.

Requires grant applications to detail plans for increasing access to data literacy and STEM education, evaluating project activities, and continuing efforts post-grant.

Mandates the use of grant funds for developing data literacy curricula, providing professional development for educators, and collaborating with industry and educational institutions.

Limits equipment purchases to 15% of grant funds annually.

Obligates recipients to submit biannual reports on grant fund usage, with data disaggregated by race, ethnicity, gender, and eligibility for free/reduced lunch.

Tasks the Secretary with submitting a comprehensive report to Congress within five years, and requires the Institute of Education Sciences to conduct a national evaluation of program effectiveness.

Authorizes $10 million annually for fiscal years 2024–2028, with caps on administrative expenses and program evaluation costs, and adds a requirement for periodic federal reporting on secondary-school STEM teachers.
