Official name: Autonomous Vehicle Accessibility Act/AV Accessibility Act
Casual name: Autonomous Vehicle Accessibility Act/AV Accessibility Act
Short summary: Prohibits states from issuing licenses for Level 4 or 5 ADS-equipped vehicle operation that discriminate against individuals with disabilities. Requires the Secretary to study infrastructure improvements for accessible ride-hail ADS-equipped vehicles. Authorizes $5 million for this study.
Long summary:
Cites the Act as the “Autonomous Vehicle Accessibility Act” or the “AV Accessibility Act”.

Defines key terms including “disability,” “public transportation,” and “ride-hail ADS-equipped vehicle.”

Prohibits states from issuing licenses for Level 4 or Level 5 ADS-equipped vehicles in a manner that discriminates against individuals with disabilities, in accordance with the ADA.

Mandates the Secretary of Transportation to commission a study through the National Academies to identify changes needed in public transportation infrastructure to improve accessibility for individuals with disabilities using ride-hail ADS-equipped vehicles.

Authorizes $5,000,000 for the completion of the accessibility infrastructure study, available until expended.

Official name: Healthy Technology Act of 2023
Casual name: Healthy Technology Act of 2023
Short summary: Authorizes artificial intelligence and machine learning technologies to prescribe pharmaceutical drugs if approved by the State involved and the Food and Drug Administration
Long summary:
Amends the definition of 'practitioner licensed by law to administer such a drug' in Section 503(b) of the Federal Food, Drug and Cosmetic Act, to include artificial intelligence and machine learning technology

Authorizes AI and ML technology to prescribe pharmaceutical drugs if approved by the State in question, and if approved by the Food and Drug Administration 

Requires such AI and ML technology to be approved by the Food and Drug Administration through conventional channels, including having to provide 90 days' premarket notification if this is deemed a new device
Tags: Applications: Medicine, life sciences and public health;Strategies: Evaluation

Official name: To establish a Joint Autonomy Office in the Department of Defense, and for other purposes.
Casual name: HR3168, 2023, Joint Autonomy Office Establishment
Short summary: Establishes the Joint Autonomy Office in the Department of Defense, responsible for coordinating and accelerating the adoption of all-domain autonomous systems for operational users, and for developing a Department-wide classification framework for autonomous capabilities.
Long summary:
Directs the Secretary of Defense to establish the Joint Autonomy Office in the Department of Defense, responsible for coordinating and accelerating the adoption of all-domain autonomous systems for operational users, including through the adoption of commercial and defense best practices

Requires the Office to implement an all-domain enterprise autonomous systems software development and testing platform across DoD's classification levels, including an all-domain data acquisition and curation process to build and manage datasets; these datasets and platforms should be released to various services and acquisition programs as they mature.

Requires the Secretary of Defense to ensure that the Office has unlimited access to existing datasets regardless of classification level, and to introduce security protocols to protect data shared with or maintained by the Office.

Requires the Director of the Office must establish a Department-wide classification framework for autonomous capabilities, considering the complexity of autonomous systems, the necessity of human supervision or control, their applicability to various warfighting domains, and costs; the Director must submit this framework along with an explanation of its criteria and requirements to Congress, with updates released no less frequently than once every two years.

Requires the Director to develop and implement plans to standardize planning, resource and integration of autonomous capabilities across the Department; this includes a report assessing the Department's current efforts which identifies acquisition, training and evaluation gaps, as well as recommendations to address these.

Official name: Disincentivizing Internet Service Censorship of Online Users and Restrictions on Speech and Expression Act
Casual name: DISCOURSE Act
Short summary: Amends Section 230 to limit liability protections for dominant service providers. Deems providers as content creators if they manage content or use targeted algorithms with a discernible viewpoint. Excludes protections for actions against religious material. Mandates public disclosure of content management practices. Reclassifies immunity as an affirmative defense.
Long summary:
Amends Section 230 of the Communications Act of 1934 to limit liability protections for dominant interactive computer service providers based on their content management practices.

Deems providers as content creators if they engage in content moderation or use targeted algorithmic amplification that expresses, promotes, or suppresses a discernible viewpoint.

Excludes liability protections for actions against religious material that burden the exercise of religion.

Mandates public disclosure of content management mechanisms and practices by providers to enable informed consumer choices and entrepreneurial activities.

Reclassifies immunity under Section 230 as an affirmative defense, requiring providers or users to prove they are not information content providers in legal actions.
Tags: Incentives: Civil liability

Official name: Advisory for AI-Generated Content Act
Casual name: Advisory for AI-Generated Content Act
Short summary: Requires all entities that generate AI materials for public consumption to label them with watermarks.
Long summary:
Requires all AI-generated materials to be labeled with a watermark. 

Authorizes the federal government to establish requirements and standards for watermarks.

Enables the federal government to levy penalties against entities that violate the above stipulation.

Defines key terms including AI-generating entity, AI-generated material, and covered AI-generated material, specifying criteria for materials that must be watermarked.

Official name: AI LEAD Act
Casual name: AI LEAD Act
Short summary: Establishes the CAIO Council to coordinate federal AI practices and risk management. Mandates each agency appoint a CAIO and requires AI Governance Boards in agencies.
Long summary:
Establishes the Chief Artificial Intelligence Officers (CAIO) Council to coordinate federal AI practices, interagency activities, and risk management. Mandates each federal agency to appoint a Chief Artificial Intelligence Officer responsible for promoting AI innovation, policy development, and risk management.

Requires the creation of Artificial Intelligence Governance Boards within each agency to oversee AI issues and ensure compliance with relevant laws and standards. Obligates agencies to develop AI strategies emphasizing trustworthy adoption, risk mitigation, transparency, and the protection of individual rights. Directs the Comptroller General to report on the effectiveness of the AI Governance Boards and CAIOs within two years of enactment.

Official name: AI Foundation Model Transparency Act of 2023
Casual name: AI Foundation Model Transparency Act of 2023
Short summary: Directs the Federal Trade Commission to issue transparency regulations for AI model training data and algorithms in consultation with relevant stakeholders within 9 months. Specifies the information categories to be publicly disclosed and updated annually.
Long summary:
Directs the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to establish and routinely update standards for making training data and algorithms of AI foundation models publicly available.

Requires the FTC to promulgate regulations within 9 months and specifies information to be disclosed, including sources of training data and model performance evaluations.

Mandates consultation with relevant stakeholders, including NIST, OSTP, and the Register of Copyrights, in establishing these standards.

Treats violations of these regulations as violations under the FTC Act regarding unfair or deceptive acts or practices.

Authorizes $10 million for fiscal year 2025 and $3 million annually thereafter for implementation.

Official name: Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2024
Casual name: Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2024
Short summary: Requires the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency to submit a report to Congress on the environmental impacts of artificial intelligence
Long summary:
Requires the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to study  the environmental impacts of artificial intelligence, with a report on its findings submitted to Congress within two years of the Act's enactment.

Mandates that the EPA study examine the energy, water consumption, and pollution throughout the full lifecycle of AI models, hardware, and data centers, considering the effects of location, power sources, and AI model design on these environmental impacts.

Requires that the EPA study assess the positive environmental impacts associated with the use of AI, such as optimizing systems for energy efficiency or automatically monitoring environmental changes.

Directs the Director of the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) to work with other government officials to form a consortium comprising academic, civil society, and industry representatives to assess and report on AI's environmental impacts, including the feasibility of open-source tools for measurement and recommendations to mitigate negative environmental effects.

Instructs the Director of NIST to work with other government officials and the consortium to establish a voluntary reporting system for entities to report on their AI systems' environmental impact.

Requires the Administrator of EPA, Secretary of Energy and Director of NIST to submit a joint report on the consortium's findings and its proposed voluntary reporting system within four years of the Act's enactment.

Official name: A bill to waive immunity under section 230 of the Communications Act of 1934 for claims and charges related to generative artificial intelligence.
Casual name: General AI Communications Act Immunity
Short summary: Removes Section 230 protection (federal liability protection) for providers of interactive computer services if the conduct giving rise to liability involves generative artificial intelligence.
Long summary: Amends Section 230 of the Communications Act of 1934 (which prevents providers of an interactive computer service from being held legally liable for third-party content) to remove this protection for claims involving the use or provision of generative artificial intelligence.

Official name: United States-Israel Health Technologies Cooperation Act
Casual name: US-Israel Health Technologies Act
Short summary: Authorizes $4,000,000 annually (2024-2026) for a bilateral health tech program with Israel emphasizing AI, drugs, vaccinations, and telemedicine. Requires matching Israeli contributions.
Long summary:
Authorizes $4,000,000 annually for fiscal years 2024 through 2026 to fund a bilateral cooperative program with Israel for developing health technologies. Emphasizes advancing technology, personalized medicine, and data usage in relation to emerging infectious diseases.

Includes artificial intelligence, drugs, vaccinations, respiratory assist devices, diagnostic tests, and telemedicine as eligible health technologies. Requires matching contributions from the Government of Israel to access funding. Allows funding for existing bilateral programs or the establishment of new programs in consultation with the Secretary of State under the 2008 Science and Technology Cooperation Agreement.

Official name: Protecting the Information of our Vulnerable Adolescents, Children, and Youth Act
Casual name: Kids PRIVACY Act
Short summary: Defines "process" as an operation, potentially automated, performed on "covered information" including collecting, disclosing, inferring, etc. Mandates "verifiable consent" before processing of data, data minimization, data access transparency, security measures, and limits to harmful practices.
Long summary:
Defines "process" as an operation, potentially automated, performed on "covered information" including collecting, disclosing, inferring, etc.

Mandates obtaining "verifiable consent" from teenagers or parents before processing their data.

Requires operators to minimize data collection and retain data only as necessary.

Obligates transparency and the provision of a clear privacy policy detailing data practices.

Prohibits targeted advertising and harmful data processing practices.

Establishes rights for teenagers and parents to access, correct, and delete data.

Imposes strict security measures for processing children's and teenagers' data.

Official name: FinCEN Modernization Act of 2023
Casual name: FinCEN Modernization Act of 2023
Short summary: Cites the Act as the "FinCEN Modernization Act of 2023." Requires FinCEN to establish research and information-sharing programs on technological advances. Mandates exploration of beneficial innovations. Obligates adoption of necessary technologies. Enables information exchange. Authorizes non-standard transactions. Compels annual reporting to Congress.
Long summary:
Cites the Act as the "FinCEN Modernization Act of 2023."

Requires FinCEN to establish research, development, and information-sharing programs focusing on technological advances like machine learning and data analytics.

Mandates FinCEN to explore and evaluate technological innovations beneficial for financial systems and detect illicit activities.

Obligates FinCEN to adopt necessary technologies for monitoring cryptocurrencies and addressing money laundering and cyber threats.

Enables FinCEN to exchange information with other Treasury offices and federal departments.

Authorizes FinCEN to enter into non-standard transactions and accept contributions, gifts, or donations to support technological advancements.

Compels FinCEN to submit an annual report detailing transactions, contributions, and their justifications and purposes to relevant Congressional committees.
Tags: Strategies: Government support: For R&D

Official name: Eliminating Bias in Algorithmic Systems Act of 2023
Casual name: Eliminating Bias in Algorithmic Systems Act of 2023
Short summary: Requires agencies that use, fund, or oversee algorithms to establish civil rights offices with bias experts. Mandates biennial reports to Congress on algorithmic bias, risks and actions taken to mitigate them.
Long summary:
Requires agencies which use or oversee a covered algorithm to have an office of civil rights employing experts and technologists on bias, discrimination, and algorithmic harms.

Mandates these offices to submit biennial reports to Congress detailing risks from use of algorithms using artificial intelligence techniques within the agency's jurisdiction, mitigation steps, actions taken to engage stakeholders, and recommendations for further mitigation of algorithmic bias and discrimination.

Establishes an interagency working group on covered algorithms and civil rights, led by the Department of Justice's Civil Rights Division.

Official name: AI Disclosure Act of 2023
Casual name: AI Disclosure Act of 2023
Short summary: Requires generative AI to include a disclaimer on outputs and subjects violators to penalties and grants privileges.
Long summary:
Requires generative artificial intelligence to include a disclaimer on any output. Mandates the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to enforce this disclosure requirement.

Classifies violations of the disclosure requirement as unfair or deceptive acts or practices under the Federal Trade Commission Act.Empowers the FTC to enforce the disclosure requirement using the same means, jurisdiction, powers, and duties as provided in the Federal Trade Commission Act. Subjects violators to penalties and grants them privileges and immunities in accordance with the Federal Trade Commission Act.

Official name: Federal Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Act of 2023
Casual name: Federal Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Act of 2023
Short summary: Requires the Director of the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) and the Director of the Office of Management and Budget to issue guidance on how federal agencies can use NIST’s AI Risk Management Framework to reduce risks.
Long summary:
Requires the Director of the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) and Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to issue guidance on how federal agencies can use NIST’s AI Risk Management Framework (RMF) to reduce risks, covering cybersecurity for AI systems, requirements for AI suppliers, and training for agencies.

Requires the Comptroller General of the United States to assess the impact of NIST's AI RMF on agencies' AI use within a year, and requires the Director of OMB to report to Congress on agency implementation and adherence to NIST’s AI RMF within a year, and subsequently, at least once every three years.

Excludes national security systems from the requirements above to adhere to NIST’s AI RMF.

Requires the Federal Acquisition Regulatory Council to issue guidance on agency AI procurement within a year, to ensure compliance with NIST’s AI RMF.

Requires the Director of OMB to establish an initiative to build agencies’ AI expertise, focusing on hiring interdisciplinary experts and incorporating best practices from existing initiatives.

Requires the Director of NIST to conduct a study on and convene stakeholders to develop voluntary consensus standards to test and evaluate AI acquisitions, develop methods and conduct evaluations, monitor evaluations and adjust methods, and make recommendations to each agency on risks from relevant AI acquisitions.

Official name: CONSULT Act of 2023
Casual name: CONSULT Act
Short summary: Prohibits the Department of Defense from awarding contracts to any individual or entity, including those producing AI-powered products and services, that has entered into working agreements with any organization from a blacklist of national security threats.
Long summary:
Requires the Department of Defense to refrain from awarding contracts to any individual or entity, including those producing AI-powered products and services, that has worked with any organization from a blacklist of national security threats.

Authorizes the DOD to punish any contractor of the Department who has a proven conflict of interest with the DOD and fails to disclose that conflict of interest.

Official name: Political BIAS Emails Act of 2023
Casual name: Political BIAS Emails Act of 2023
Short summary: Prohibits email providers from flagging elected political emails as spam without user direction. Requires quarterly transparency reports on flagged political emails. Mandates data disclosure to campaigns upon request. Obliges providers to offer email deliverability best practices. Enforces via FTC. Defines key terms.
Long summary:
Prohibits email service providers from using filtering algorithms to flag emails from political campaigns that consumers have elected to receive as spam without user direction.

Requires providers to publish quarterly transparency reports detailing instances of political emails flagged as spam, including breakdowns by political party and filtering algorithm usage.

Mandates email providers to disclose specific spam-filtering data to political campaigns upon request, with limitations on request frequency.

Obliges providers to offer best practices to political campaigns for improving email deliverability to primary inboxes.

Enforces these provisions through the Federal Trade Commission, treating violations as unfair or deceptive acts under the Federal Trade Commission Act.

Defines key terms including “filtering algorithm,” “operator,” and “political campaign.”

Official name: Promoting Resilient Supply Chains Act of 2023
Casual name: Promoting Resilient Supply Chains Act
Short summary: Requires the Assistant Secretary of Commerce to promote U.S. leadership in AI and strengthen supply chains. Mandates a resiliency program. Requires coordination with agencies. Promotes domestic manufacturing. Supports best practices. Mandates regular reporting on AI supply chains.
Long summary:
Requires the Assistant Secretary of Commerce to promote U.S. leadership in emerging technologies, including artificial intelligence (AI), and strengthen critical supply chains and national security.

Mandates the establishment of a critical supply chain resiliency and crisis response program within the Department of Commerce, which includes mapping and monitoring AI supply chains.

Requires coordination with other agencies, industry, and international partners to identify vulnerabilities and prepare for supply chain shocks affecting AI technologies.

Promotes the growth of domestic manufacturing capacities for AI and other emerging technologies and encourages the relocation of manufacturing facilities to the U.S. or allied countries.

Supports the development and dissemination of best practices for supply chain resilience, including the use of blockchain and other technologies to authenticate and trace critical goods.

Mandates regular reporting on the state of AI and other emerging technology supply chains, including assessments of vulnerabilities and recommendations for enhancing resilience.

Official name: DATA Privacy Act
Casual name: DATA Privacy Act
Short summary: Establishes national data privacy standards for covered entities. Requires clear privacy notices. Mandates FTC regulations within one year. Imposes data minimization requirements. Empowers individuals with data rights. Mandates privacy officers for large entities. Grants FTC and state enforcement authority. Allocates FTC resources.
Long summary:
Establishes national data privacy standards for covered entities handling personal data.

Requires covered entities to post clear, accessible privacy notices detailing data collection, processing, storage, and disclosure practices.

Mandates the FTC to create regulations within one year for reasonable, equitable, and non-deceptive data practices, including opt-out and affirmative consent mechanisms.

Imposes data minimization requirements, limiting data collection to what is necessary for specified purposes and ensuring timely deletion.

Empowers individuals with rights to access, correct, delete, and transfer their data.

Mandates large entities to appoint privacy protection officers to oversee compliance and advocate for privacy policies.

Directs the National Science Foundation to support research on privacy-enhancing technologies.

Grants enforcement authority to the FTC and state attorneys general, with civil penalties for violations.

Allocates resources to the FTC for enforcement and compliance activities.

Official name: Outbound Investment Transparency Act of 2023
Casual name: Outbound Investment Transparency Act
Short summary: Amends the Defense Production Act to establish an investment screening mechanism targeting AI and other sensitive sectors. Requires U.S. persons to notify the Treasury of certain investments in countries of concern. Mandates annual reporting and promotes international coordination.
Long summary:
Requires mandatory notification by U.S. persons engaging in specific foreign investments related to advanced sectors, including artificial intelligence, to the Secretary of the Treasury.

Defines covered activities involving equity interests, debt obligations with governance rights, and joint ventures in countries of concern, with specific focus on AI and other advanced technologies.

Establishes penalties for non-compliance, including failure to notify or providing false information.

Mandates the creation of regulations to ensure clarity and minimize compliance burdens, with a focus on transparency and stakeholder involvement.

Directs the Secretary of State to engage with allied countries to coordinate investment screening mechanisms and share information on covered activities.

Requires annual reporting to Congress on trends, national security risks, and recommendations related to these foreign investments in AI and other sectors.

Official name: TEST AI Act of 2023
Casual name: TEST AI Act of 2023
Short summary: Requires the NIST Director to establish testbeds to support the development of trustworthy AI systems, examine vulnerabilities in existing systems, and build guardrails for harm prevention.
Long summary:
Requires the Director of the National Institute on Standards and Technology (NIST) to work with other heads of federal agencies and private/public sector stakeholders to build testbeds for supporting the development of trustworthy AI systems and AI guardrails.

Mandates the Secretary of Commerce and Secretary of Energy to enter into a memorandum of understanding that allows relevant agencies across the federal government access to AI-related personnel, facilities, and research at the Department of Energy.

Official name: AI Leadership Training Act
Casual name: AI Leadership Training Act
Short summary: Establishes an AI leadership training program for executive agencies. Requires annual training on AI capabilities, risks, and ethics. Mandates topics cover AI basics, risks, and constitutional issues. Obligates updates biennially. Demands participation metrics. Permits Congressional oversight. Sets a 10-year sunset clause.
Long summary:
Establishes an AI leadership training program for management officials, supervisors, and other designated employees in executive agencies, to be developed and implemented within 18 months.

Requires annual training to cover AI capabilities, risks, safety, ethics, Federal Government requirements, and best practices related to AI procurement, use, testing, evaluation, and auditing.

Mandates program topics include AI basics, benefits, risks, data significance, AI system failures, continuous refinement, risk mitigation, organizational considerations, and constitutional risks related to AI use.

Obligates updates to the program every two years to incorporate new AI information and ensure compliance with training requirements.

Demands metrics to measure participation and feedback for program improvements.

Permits Congress to request detailed information about the program materials and responsible personnel.

Encourages interaction with experts from various sectors as part of the training.

Sets a sunset clause for the program 10 years after enactment.

Official name: Water Quality and Environmental Innovation Act
Casual name: Water Quality and Environmental Innovation Act
Short summary: Authorizes the EPA Administrator to award grants and contracts for emerging technologies addressing water quality. Mandates the use of AI, quantum information science, and robotics for water quality projects.
Long summary:
Authorizes the Environmental Protection Agency's (EPA) Administrator to award grants and contracts for projects using emerging technologies to address water quality threats.

Establishes the Water Quality and Environmental Innovation Fund, funding it through fiscal years 2024-2028 using fees from the EPA's Motor Vehicle and Engine Compliance Program.

Mandates the use of technologies, including artificial intelligence, quantum information science, and robotics, among others, for water quality projects.

Requires that project results, including data and statistics, be publicly accessible.

Directs the EPA Administrator to report annually to Congress on the benefits, recipients, goals, and effectiveness of funded projects, starting one year after the Act's enactment.

Official name: Creating Resources for Every American To Experiment with Artificial Intelligence Act of 2023
Casual name: CREATE AI Act of 2023

Official name: Ensuring Safe and Ethical AI Development Through SAFE AI Research Grants
Casual name: SAFE AI Research Grants
Short summary: Requires the National Academy of Sciences to establish a grant program for the development of safe AI models and AI research; directs and outlines the submission of a proposal for the program in accordance with guiding principles.
Long summary:
Requires the National Academy of Sciences to establish a grant program for the development of safe AI models and AI research.

Mandates the development of guiding principles and ethical considerations for the grant program.

Directs the Director of the National Academy of Sciences to submit a detailed proposal of the grant program to relevant Congressional committees within one year, including a budget, proposed appropriation request, and an evaluation of existing AI models and methodologies.

Official name: National Critical Capabilities Defense Act of 2023
Casual name: National Critical Capabilities Defense Act
Short summary: Defines "national critical capability sector" as including artificial intelligence and defines "covered activities" as being related to "national critical capability sectors". Establishes the "Committee on National Critical Capabilities" to review U.S. investments abroad threatening national security.
Long summary:
Defines "national critical capabilities sector" as sectors, including artificial intelligence, subject to regulation described in the bill.

Establishes the "Committee on National Critical Capabilities" to review U.S. investments in foreign countries that may threaten national security.

Requires U.S. persons to notify the Committee of covered activities in critical sectors, including artificial intelligence, at least 90 days before initiation.

Instructs the Committee to review and potentially mitigate risks posed by such activities, with authority to prohibit or regulate investments.

Defines "covered activities" broadly to include acquisitions, joint ventures, and other engagements with foreign entities in critical sectors.

Identifies countries of concern, including China, Russia, and North Korea, where such investments are scrutinized more closely.

Grants the Committee authority to enforce compliance, including imposing civil penalties up to $250,000 or twice the amount of the covered activity.

Official name: National AI Commission Act
Casual name: National AI Commission Act
Short summary: Establishes the National AI Commission, an independent and bi-partisan commission in the legislative branch responsible for reviewing and recommending measures to mitigate AI risks and protect US leadership in AI development
Long summary:
Establishes the National AI Commission, an independent and bi-partisan commission in the legislative branch responsible for reviewing and recommending measures for the US to mitigate the risks and possible harms of AI, and to ensure US leadership in AI innovation and development.

Directs the National AI Commission to review the Federal Government's existing approach to AI oversight and regulation, including the existing distribution of oversight and regulatory authorities across various agencies, and to recommend governmental structures that may be needed to regulate powerful general-purpose AI systems.

Instruct the National AI Commission to submit an interim report to Congress and the President within six months, a final report with its findings and recommendations for a binding regulatory framework no later than six months after the interim report, and a follow-up report within one year of the final report's submission.

Requires the National AI Commission to comprise twenty commissioners appointed by the President and by Congressional leadership, with ten appointed by each party and co-chaired by a Democratic appointee and a Republican appointee.

Official name: Closing Loopholes for the Overseas Use and Development of Artificial Intelligence Act
Casual name: CLOUD AI Act
Short summary: Prohibits US persons and subsidiaries from supporting the use of remote or cloud integrated circuits by entities in China.
Long summary: Grants authority to the Secretary of Commerce to prohibit US persons and subsidiaries from supporting the use of remote or cloud integrated circuits listed under Export Control Classification Number 3A090 and 4A090 of the Export Administration Regulations by entities located in the People’s Republic of China or Macau.

Official name: MEANS Act
Casual name: MEANS Act
Short summary: Establishes the Office of Manufacturing Security and Resilience to enhance supply chain resilience, focusing on critical goods. Supports domestic manufacturing, reduces reliance on concerning countries, and incentivizes relocation of facilities. Provides loans, loan guarantees, and sets criteria for eligible entities.
Long summary:
Establishes an Office of Manufacturing Security and Resilience within the Department of Commerce to oversee the Manufacturing Security and Resilience Program.

Mandates the Under Secretary to develop and implement a strategy to strengthen supply chains for critical goods, reducing reliance on concentrated supply chains, particularly from countries of concern.

Requires collaboration with other federal agencies to support allies and key international partners in building manufacturing capacity for critical goods.

Incentivizes domestic manufacturers to relocate production from countries of concern and increase production capacity, using loans, loan guarantees, and other financial supports.

Prioritizes support for activities that enhance the resilience, security, and diversity of supply chains and address supply chain shocks.

Mandates periodic submission of strategy reports and program evaluations to Congress, with updates every four years.

Allocates $35 billion for fiscal years 2024-2028 to fund the program, with specific allocations for loans, loan guarantees, and administrative costs.

Official name: Stop Fentanyl at the Border Act
Casual name: Stop Fentanyl at the Border Act
Short summary:
Appropriates $1,090,000,000 to U.S. Customs and Border Protection for technology improvements to enhance fentanyl interdiction.

Appropriates $223,000,000 to U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement to expand fentanyl interdiction and disrupt transnational criminal networks.

Mandates submission of reports by March 31, 2028, on impacts of Border Patrol Processing Coordinator positions and equipment needs.

Creates penalties for damaging or evading border controls, with fines and imprisonment up to 5 years for violations aimed at financial gain or furthering criminal organizations.
Long summary:
Appropriates $1,090,000,000 to U.S. Customs and Border Protection for technology improvements, including non-intrusive inspection scanning systems and anomaly detection algorithms, to enhance fentanyl interdiction at land borders.

Appropriates $223,000,000 to U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement to expand efforts to interdict fentanyl and disrupt transnational criminal networks, including funds for additional special agents and joint surge operations.

Mandates submission of reports by March 31, 2028, detailing impacts of Border Patrol Processing Coordinator positions, equipment needs for outbound inspections, and summaries of non-intrusive inspection technology at land ports of entry.

Creates penalties for knowingly damaging or evading border controls, with fines and imprisonment up to 5 years for violations aimed at securing financial gain or furthering criminal organizations.

Official name: No ICBMs or Drones for Iran Act of 2023
Casual name: No ICBMs or Drones for Iran Act of 2023
Short summary: Expands and incorporates sanctions related to Iranian unmanned aerial systems (UAS). Mandates a strategy against, and reports on support for, Iran’s UAS program.
Long summary:
Expands sanctions under the Comprehensive Iran Sanctions, Accountability, and Divestment Act of 2010 to include unmanned aerial systems (UAS).

Incorporates UAS under arms embargos of the Countering America’s Adversaries Through Sanctions Act and Iran-Iraq Arms Non-Proliferation Act of 1992.

Requires the President to submit a strategy to counter Iran's UAS program including export controls of US materials, identification of actors supporting Iran's UAS program, assessment of compliance with such restrictions, and coordination with US allies in furtherance of these actions

Mandates a report on support for Iran’s UAS program from domestic industries, individuals, or entities in Iran and related technology transfers to foreign actors, to be updated as necessary.

Official name: Protect Elections from Deceptive AI Act
Casual name: Protect Elections from Deceptive AI Act
Short summary: Prohibits knowingly distributing materially deceptive AI-generated media related to Federal elections.
Long summary:
Prohibits knowingly distributing materially deceptive AI-generated audio or visual media related to Federal election activities or candidates. Exempts broadcasters, streaming services, and periodicals if they disclose authenticity concerns and covers bona fide news or satire. 

Allows affected candidates to seek injunctive relief or damages, including attorney's fees, for violations. Requires plaintiffs to prove violations with clear and convincing evidence.

Casual name: Artificial Intelligence Research, Innovation, and Accountability Act of 2023
Short summary: Develops standards for online content verification, mandates best practices for AI detection, reviews government AI use, prohibits generative AI without user notice, requires transparency reports, and establishes oversight, risk assessments, certification, enforcement, and consumer education for AI.
Long summary:
Requires the Under Secretary of Commerce to develop standards for online content verification. Mandates best practices for detecting AI outputs. Directs review of AI usage in government. Prohibits generative AI without user notification. 
Requires AI transparency reports. Instructs NIST on AI oversight. Obligates risk assessments for critical-impact AI. Establishes AI Certification Advisory Committee. Empowers enforcement of compliance. Establishes consumer education working group.

Official name: Tornado Observations Research and Notification Assessment for Development of Operations Act
Casual name: TORNADO Act
Short summary: Requires the Under Secretary of Commerce to improve NOAA's communication system for hazardous events using methods such data analysis supported by artificial intelligence and machine learning technologies.
Long summary:
Requires the Under Secretary of Commerce for Oceans and Atmosphere to improve the communication system of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) for hazardous weather and water events to prevent loss of life and property.

Mandates the establishment of a hazard risk communication office to simplify and improve communication, using the latest social, behavioral, risk, and communication science research, including AI and machine learning technologies.

Instructs the Under Secretary to create a research program to modernize risk-based hazard communication and establish a pilot program for tornado hazard communication.

Official name: Disrupt Explicit Forged Images And Non-Consensual Edits Act of 2024
Casual name: DEFIANCE Act of 2024
Short summary: Amends the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2022, to define "digital forgery" as AI-created intimate visual depictions. Allows civil action for non-consensual digital forgeries. Specifies damages and relief. Permits privacy protections. Establishes a 10-year statute of limitations. Ensures non-preemption.
Long summary:
Amends the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2022, to include "digital forgery" as any intimate visual depiction created via AI or other technological means to appear indistinguishable from an authentic visual depiction.

Allows identifiable individuals to bring civil action against those who produce, possess, disclose, or solicit digital forgeries without consent, with knowledge or reckless disregard of the lack of consent.

Specifies relief for plaintiffs, including actual or liquidated damages of $150,000, attorney’s fees, and equitable relief such as injunctions.

Permits courts to issue orders to protect plaintiffs' privacy, including the use of pseudonyms and sealing personal identifying information.

Establishes a 10-year statute of limitations for actions, starting from the discovery of the violation or the individual reaching 18 years of age.

Ensures the Act does not preempt state, federal, or tribal laws that provide at least as much protection.

Official name: Preventing Algorithmic Collusion Act of 2024
Casual name: Preventing Algorithmic Collusion Act of 2024
Short summary: Prohibits using pricing algorithms trained with nonpublic competitor data. Requires detailed reporting and certification of pricing algorithms used in commerce. Mandates transparency in algorithmic pricing for entities with over $5 million revenue. Establishes penalties for violations. Directs FTC to study pricing algorithms' impact.
Long summary:
Prohibits the use of pricing algorithms incorporating nonpublic competitor data.

Requires entities using or distributing pricing algorithms to submit detailed reports to the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) or Attorney General upon request, detailing development, data usage, and algorithmic rules.

Mandates certification of report accuracy by a high-level corporate officer.

Ensures confidentiality of submitted reports, allowing information sharing only between the Department of Justice, FTC, and National Institute of Standards and Technology.

Imposes civil penalties for violations, including fines and injunctive relief.

Presumes collusive behavior in antitrust cases involving certain uses of pricing algorithms, allowing for rebuttal and assigning joint liability.

Obligates disclosure to customers and employees when prices or terms are set by algorithms, including specifics on price discrimination and third-party algorithm developers.

Directs the FTC to study and report on the impact and prevalence of pricing algorithms within two years, including potential consumer harm and benefits.

Official name: Building Resilient Supply Chains Act
Casual name: Building Resilient Supply Chains Act
Short summary: Directs the Department of Commerce to establish a Supply Chain Resilience and Crisis Response Office to administer grants and loans to support domestic manufacturing and supply chain diversification for in key technology areas, including artificial intelligence.
Long summary:
Directs the Department of Commerce to establish a Supply Chain Resiliency and Crisis Response Office, responsible for awarding grants, loans, and loan guarantees to critical industry and supply chain stakeholders that integrate artificial intelligence into manufacturing.

Mandates the DOC to improve the competitiveness of the advanced tech manufacturing sector, minimize supply chain shocks, and support the creation of related jobs in key technology focus areas such as artificial intelligence, machine learning, and quantum computing.

Directs the Inspector General of the DOC to audit activities supported by the Supply Chain Resiliency and Crisis Response Office's grants, loans, and loan guarantees every four years.

Official name: Artificial Intelligence Accountability Act
Casual name: Artificial Intelligence Accountability Act
Short summary: Requires the Assistant Secretary of Commerce for Communications and Information to conduct a study on accountability mechanisms to provide assurances that an artificial intelligence (AI) system is trustworthy and hold public meetings to gather feedback on the disclosure of information about AI systems.
Long summary:
Requires the Assistant Secretary of Commerce for Communications and Information to study accountability mechanisms (e.g., audits, certifications, and assessments) that provide assurances that an AI system is trustworthy. This includes defining terms around AI “trustworthiness,” examining how mechanisms are incorporated into AI systems, and assessing their ability to reduce risks related to AI systems, such as cybersecurity threats. 

Requires the Assistant Secretary to solicit stakeholder feedback through public meetings while carrying out the study above and to report the study’s findings to Congress, including recommendations on effective accountability measures for AI systems. 

Requires the Assistant Secretary to consult with stakeholders through public meetings and report to Congress on what information should be disclosed to those who interact with, are affected by, or study AI systems, and the most effective methods for doing so.

Casual name: R U REAL Act
Short summary: Directs the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to amend the Telemarketing Sales Rule to require the disclosure of artificial intelligence usage in telemarketing.
Long summary:
Directs the FTC to revise the Telemarketing Sales Rule to require the disclosure of AI usage to impersonate a human being in telemarketing.

Mandates the FTC to impose financial penalties on violators of this new rule, especially towards individuals and entities with the intent to defraud, cause harm, or wrongfully obtain anything of value through AI telemarketing techniques.

Casual name: STOP HATE Act
Short summary: Mandates social media companies to submit terms of service reports to the Attorney General's office that provide data on how artificial intelligence is being used to flag content and manage disinformation.
Long summary:
Requires social media companies to make their terms of service publicly available.

Requires social media companies to disclose content moderation data, including the total number of flagged items, actioned items, user engagement, and how artificial intelligence is used to support content moderation efforts.

Allows Attorney General's office to bring a civil penalty of $5,000,000 per violation per day against social media companies who fail to meet these requirements

Long summary: Requires a report on artificial intelligence regulation in the financial services industry, establishes artificial intelligence bug bounty programs, requires a vulnerability analysis study for artificial intelligence-enabled military applications, and requires a report on data sharing and coordination.

Casual name: QUIET Act
Short summary: Directs Congress to amend the Communications Act of 1934 to require disclosure of the use of artificial intelligence in voice call or text message impersonations.
Long summary:
Directs Congress to amend the Communications Act of 1934 to mandate the disclosure of artificial intelligence usage in voice call or text message impersonations.

Mandates the imposition of financial penalties on those who fail to complete the required disclosure of artificial intelligence usage during robocalls.

Official name: ASSESS AI Act
Casual name: ASSESS AI Act
Short summary: Establishes a task force to assess AI's privacy, civil rights, and civil liberties implications; outlines duties and responsibilities of task force.
Long summary:
Establishes a task force to assess privacy, civil rights, and civil liberties implications of artificial intelligence. 

Charges the task force to evaluate existing policy, regulatory, and legal gaps for AI applications and make legislative and regulatory recommendations.

Directs the task force to address the application of federal antidiscrimination laws, disparate impact standards, AI validation, and auditing in federal use of AI.

Instructs the task force to consider institutional changes for ongoing AI governance and the potential creation of a new oversight organization.

Requires the task force to submit an interim report within one year and a final report within 18 months.

Official name: Preventing Deep Fake Scams Act
Casual name: Preventing Deep Fake Scams Act
Short summary: Establishes a task force to help formulate appropriate AI regulations in the financial services industry and requires the task force to produce a report for Congress with these recommendations within one year.
Long summary:
Establishes a task force examining the fraudulent use of artificial intelligence in the financial services sector.

Instruct the task force to solicit public feedback from industry and expert stakeholders, including depository institutions, credit unions, third-party vendors, and AI experts.

Directs the task force to produce a report for Congress within one year that identifies potential risks from AI misuse, and proposes AI fraud detection measures, among other legislative recommendations.

Terminates the task force 90 days after the final report's issue.

Official name: Farm Tech Act
Casual name: Farm Tech Act
Short summary: Mandates the Secretary of Agriculture to establish a program to certify AI software for agricultural use based on the NIST's Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework.
Long summary:
Mandates the Secretary of Agriculture to establish a program to certify AI software for the production of agricultural products. 

Bases the certification program on the  National Institute of Standards and Technology's Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework.

Permits certification only if the software performs accurately and meets or exceeds relevant Federal and State standards for licensure, certification, and permitting.

Details specific licensure and certification requirements for various tasks, including commercial driving, manure application, pesticide application and handling hazardous materials.
Tags: Risk factors: Reliability;Strategies: Evaluation;Strategies: Evaluation: Conformity assessment;Strategies: Governance development;Applications: Agriculture and resource extraction

Official name: Investing in Tomorrow's Workforce Act of 2023
Casual name: Investing in Tomorrow's Workforce Act of 2023
Short summary: Allocates $40 million annually for dislocated worker grants aimed at training workers displaced by automation. Prioritizes partnerships in high-risk areas. Mandates outcome reports.
Long summary:
Authorizes the Secretary of Labor to award competitive grants to support projects addressing the training needs of workers likely to be displaced by automation.

Prioritizes grants for partnerships in areas with high concentrations of affected populations or industries susceptible to automation.

Requires eligible partnerships to provide training services, develop curricula, and offer employment benefits, such as transportation stipends and paid leave.

Mandates grant recipients to submit comprehensive reports on training outcomes, including transitions to new positions and earnings data.

Expands the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act to include training for workers impacted by automation.

Allocates $40 million annually from 2024 to 2028 for national dislocated worker grants to address impacts of automation.

Ensures grant usage aligns with labor standards and nondiscrimination provisions under the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act.

Official name: AI Training Expansion Act of 2023
Casual name: AI Training Expansion Act of 2023
Short summary: Expand training programs for helping executive branch employees better understand the function, risks and benefits, and nature of artificial intelligence.
Long summary:
Amends the Artificial Intelligence Training for the Acquisition Workforce Act to expand artificial intelligence training within the executive branch.

Requires the training program to cover capabilities, risks and benefits, and mitigation strategies associated with AI.

Official name: Basic Allowance for Housing Calculation Improvement Act of 2023
Casual name: Basic Allowance for Housing Calculation Improvement Act of 2023
Short summary: Directs the Secretary of Defense to conduct a pilot program to use AI for calculating monthly rates of basic allowance for housing.
Long summary:
Requires the Secretary of Defense to conduct a pilot program to outsource the calculation of monthly Basic Allowance for Housing (BAH) rates using machine learning and artificial intelligence algorithms by engaging a covered entity.

Mandates the submission of a report to the Senate and House Armed Services Committees evaluating the AI-calculated rates.

Official name: Fair Trade with China Enforcement Act, Sec. 102 ("Prohibition on export to People's Republic of China of national security sensitive technology and intellectual property")
Casual name: Fair Trade with China Enforcement Act, Sec. 102 ("Prohibit export of national security sensitive technology and IP to PRC")
Short summary: Prohibits the Secretary of Commerce from exporting national security sensitive technology or intellectual property, including AI-related goods or services, to China. Defines such technology as contributing to China's military, harming U.S. security, economy, or involving critical production components.
Long summary:
Prohibits the Secretary of Commerce from exporting any national security sensitive technology or intellectual property to the People's Republic of China.

Defines "intellectual property" to include patents, copyrights, trademarks, or trade secrets.

Defines "national security sensitive technology or intellectual property" to include contributions to China's military, protection of U.S. economic security, and components of key products listed under the Trade Act of 1974.

Includes in "technology" goods or services related to information systems, internet-based services, production-enhancing logistics, robotics, artificial intelligence, biotechnology, or computing.

Official name: Kids Online Safety Act, Sec. 13 ("Filter Bubble Transparency Requirements")
Casual name: Kids Online Safety Act, Sec. 13 ("Filter Bubble Transparency")
Short summary: Requires covered internet platforms to inform users about opaque algorithm and user data usage and provide detailed algorithm operational information. Mandates offering an input-transparent algorithm alternative and prohibits discrimination against users of this alternative.
Long summary:
Requires providers of covered internet platforms that use opaque algorithms to notify users about their use and provide detailed information about their operation, including their features, user-specific data collection, and algorithmic inputs in an easily accessible form.

Mandates platforms to offer an easily accessible input-transparent algorithm alternative and prohibits discrimination, via methods like differential pricing, against users who choose the alternative.

Empowers enforcement by the Federal Trade Commission to treat violations as unfair or deceptive acts under the Federal Trade Commission Act.

Exempts small downstream providers and non-profit organizations from certain enforcement provisions.
Tags: Strategies: Input controls;Strategies: Input controls: Data circulation;Strategies: Input controls: Data use;Risk factors: Privacy;Risk factors: Transparency;Harms: Discrimination;Strategies: Disclosure;Strategies: Disclosure: In standard form;Strategies: Disclosure: About inputs;Strategies: Input controls;Strategies: Input controls: Data use;Incentives: Civil liability

Official name: Fair Trade with China Enforcement Act, Sec. 101 ("Establishment of list of certain products receiving support from government of People's Republic of China pursuant to Made in China 2025 policy")
Casual name: Fair Trade with China Enforcement Act, Sec. 101 ("List of products supported by PRC's Made in China 2025 policy")
Short summary: Requires the United States Trade Representative to annually list Chinese products, including AI, receiving government support under the Made in China 2025 policy. Identifies specific industries such as AI, robotics, and quantum computing for inclusion.
Long summary:
Amends the Trade Act of 1974 to establish a list of products supported by the Chinese government under the Made in China 2025 policy.

Requires the United States Trade Representative to compile this list within 120 days of the enactment and update it annually.

Mandates inclusion of products specified in key Chinese government documents related to Made in China 2025.

Includes products that displace net exports of similar U.S. products.

Enumerates specific industries such as civil aircraft, motor vehicles, advanced medical equipment, agricultural machinery, semiconductors, lithium batteries, artificial intelligence, high-capacity computing, quantum computing, robotics, and biotechnology for inclusion.

Official name: Black Maternal Health Omnibus Act, Sec. 904 (Report on the Use of Technology in Maternity Care)
Casual name: Black Maternal Health Omnibus Act, Sec. 904 (Report on the Use of Technology in Maternity Care)
Short summary: Mandates the Secretary of Health and Human Services to commission a study on technology in maternity care. Requires examination of AI's impact on racial and ethnic biases. Includes patient monitoring devices. Specifies identification of bias reduction practices. Directs completion within 24 months.
Long summary:
Mandates the Secretary of Health and Human Services to enter an agreement with the National Academies of Sciences, Engineering, and Medicine to study the use of technology in maternity care.

Requires the study to examine the use of innovative technology, including artificial intelligence, and its impact on racial or ethnic biases in maternal health care.

Includes patient monitoring devices, such as pulse oximeters, in the scope of the study, with a focus on their effect on racial or ethnic biases.

Outlines identification of best practices for reducing and preventing biases in the use of technology and monitoring devices in maternity care.

Requires the study to recommend best practices for using technology and monitoring devices for pregnant and postpartum individuals from minority groups.

Mandates the inclusion of best practices regarding privacy and security safeguards in the use of such technologies.

Directs the National Academies to complete the study and submit a report to Congress within 24 months of the Act's enactment.
Tags: Strategies: Government study or report;Strategies: Evaluation;Strategies: Evaluation: Post-market monitoring;Harms: Discrimination;Harms: Violation of civil or human rights, including privacy;Harms: Harm to health/safety;Risk factors: Bias;Risk factors: Privacy;Risk factors: Security;Risk factors: Safety;Applications: Medicine, life sciences and public health;Applications: Security;Strategies: Government study or report

Official name: TORPEDO Act of 2023,  Sec. 12 (Expedited release of advanced technologies to Australia, Canada, and the United Kingdom through the foreign military sales program)
Casual name: TORPEDO Act of 2023,  Sec. 12 (Expedited release of advanced technologies to Australia, Canada, and the United Kingdom through the foreign military sales program)
Short summary: Expedites the release of AI and other advanced technologies to Australia, Canada, and the UK through the Foreign Military Sales program. Establishes a fast-track decision-making process for AI capabilities, with a presumption of approval, and anticipatory release policy.
Long summary:
Requires the Secretary of State, in coordination with the Secretary of Defense, to compile a list of pre-cleared military technologies and platforms for sale to Australia, Canada, and the UK within 90 days of enactment and annually thereafter.

Expedites the processing of Foreign Military Sales requests from Australia, Canada, and the UK.

Mandates the creation of an anticipatory release policy for key Foreign Military Sales capabilities, including AI, hypersonic missiles, cyber capabilities, and quantum technologies, with a presumption of approval.

Requires the review and update of interagency policies to incorporate these anticipatory release provisions.

Official name: TORPEDO Act of 2023, Sec. 6 (Reporting requirements on efforts of the Department of State to implement the AUKUS partnership)
Casual name: TORPEDO Act of 2023, Sec. 6 (Reporting requirements on efforts of the Department of State to implement the AUKUS partnership)
Short summary: Requires submission of reports detailing interagency actions and progress in implementing advanced capabilities under the AUKUS agreement, including AI and autonomy. Assesses export control changes and collaboration efforts among AUKUS partners on AI and other technologies.
Long summary:
Mandates the Secretary of State, in coordination with the Secretaries of Defense, Commerce, and Energy, to report on the Department of State's implementation of the AUKUS partnership within 90 days of enactment.

Requires the report to detail Australia's progress on various submarine-related goals, including infrastructure, workforce training, and basing support.

Instructs the report to assess Australia's submarine contributions to U.S. defense objectives and provide a strategy for integrating AUKUS and U.S. submarines.

Mandates reporting on U.S. export control processes and recommendations for improvements to facilitate the AUKUS partnership.

Demands an interagency report on changes to ITAR and export control regimes to enable defense item exchanges among AUKUS members and Canada.

Requires an assessment of efforts to enhance collaboration in AI, quantum technologies, cyber capabilities, and other advanced defense areas.

Obligates the President to brief Congress annually for seven years on AUKUS implementation progress.

Official name: Coast Guard Authorization Act of 2023, Sec. 212 ("Report on Establishment of an Unmanned Systems Capabilities Office")
Casual name: Coast Guard Authorization Act, Sec. 212 ("Unmanned Systems Capabilities Office")
Short summary: Requires the Commandant to report on establishing a Coast Guard office for developing and acquiring unmanned and counter-unmanned systems. Details management, coordination, contracting strategies, and collaboration with federal agencies, and emphasizes integrating AI and machine learning tools in operations.
Long summary:
Requires the Commandant to submit a report within one year outlining a plan for establishing an unmanned systems capabilities office within the Coast Guard.

Mandates inclusion of a management strategy for acquiring, developing, and deploying unmanned system and counter-unmanned system technologies.

Demands a coordination strategy to synchronize efforts across the Coast Guard and pursue expanded research, development, testing, and evaluation opportunities.

Requires identification of contracting and acquisition authorities needed for expedited technology development and deployment.

Lists the need for a detailed inventory of commercially available unmanned and counter-unmanned systems useful to the Coast Guard.

Calls for a cross-agency collaboration plan with the Department of Homeland Security, Department of Defense, and other relevant agencies.

Encourages opportunities to obtain and share unmanned system data to enhance maritime domain awareness.

Mandates the development of a concept of operations for a data ecosystem integrating unmanned system technologies with AI, machine learning, and information-sharing capabilities.

Official name: Department of State Authorization Act of 2023, Sec. 304 ("Establishment of the Chief Artificial Intelligence Officer of the Department of State")
Casual name: Department of State Authorization Act, Sec. 304 ("DOS Chief AI Officer")
Short summary: Establishes a Chief Artificial Intelligence Officer within the Department of State to oversee AI adoption and advise on ethical AI use. Requires collaboration with the Chief Information Officer for scaling AI. Defines AI per the National Defense Authorization Act.
Long summary:
Establishes within the Department of State a Chief Artificial Intelligence Officer (CAIO), potentially dual-hatted as the Department’s Chief Data Officer, and a member of the Senior Executive Service.

Requires the CAIO to evaluate, oversee, and facilitate the responsible adoption of AI and machine learning to inform decisions and support programs and operations.

Designates the CAIO as the principal advisor to the Secretary of State on the ethical use of AI and advanced analytics in diplomacy.

Mandates the CAIO to possess skills in data analytics, AI, machine learning, and organizational change management.

Requires the Chief Information Officer to consult with the CAIO on scaling AI capabilities across software applications.

Defines “artificial intelligence” as per section 238(g) of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2019.

Official name: Advanced Safe Testing at Residence Telehealth Act of 2023, Sec. 2 ("Coverage and Payment for Certain Tests and Assistive Telehealth Consultations; Demonstration Program Under Certain State Medicaid Programs")
Casual name: Testing at Residence Telehealth Act, Sec. 2 ("Tests and Assistive Telehealth Consultations Demonstration")
Short summary: Establishes a Value-Based Insurance Design Model for Medicare, covering AI-driven wearable medical devices and assistive telehealth. Limits demonstration to 25 plans, promoting rural access, and requires demographic data for reimbursement. Allocates funding for state Medicaid telehealth programs.
Long summary:
Establishes a Value-Based Insurance Design Model (VBID) demonstration program to provide Medicare beneficiaries with assistive telehealth consultations and home-based care.

Requires eligible Medicare Advantage (MA) plans to cover medical tests, including those utilizing adaptive AI and machine learning, for various diseases and conditions.

Limits the VBID program to a maximum of 25 MA plans, with at least 10 plans serving rural or underserved areas.

Mandates demographic data collection for reimbursement claims, including age, race, ethnicity, gender, and chronic conditions.

Administers a grant program for state Medicaid programs to cover similar tests and telehealth consultations, with grants awarded to 5-10 states or territories for a 4-year period, not exceeding $12 million per state.

Official name: NTIA Reauthorization Act of 2023, Sec. 203 ("Spectrum management improvements")
Casual name: NTIA Reauthorization Act, Sec. 203 ("Spectrum management improvements")
Short summary: Develops and supports AI systems for advanced antenna arrays and dynamic spectrum access. Encourages workforce development and partnerships for prototyping advanced communications technologies. Identifies spectrum management technologies like dynamic spectrum access and network sensing for Federal frequency assignments.
Long summary:
Requires the Under Secretary, in coordination with the Commission and in consultation with other relevant Federal agencies, to develop, establish, prototype, and support the implementation of models, methodologies, and inputs for spectrum management decisions involving AI systems, advanced antenna arrays, and dynamic spectrum access technologies.

Mandates the development of AI systems to enable dynamic spectrum access, Internet of Things networks, and other advanced communications technologies.

Amends Section 104 of the National Telecommunications and Information Administration Organization Act to require the identification and facilitation of technologies that promote dynamic spectrum access, network sensing and monitoring, and optical and quantum communications.

Encourages the development of a skilled workforce for prototyping advanced communications technologies.

Supports partnerships among institutions for workforce development in advanced communications technology prototyping.

Official name: Department of State Authorization Act of 2023, Sec. 303 ("Task force to address artificial intelligence-enabled influence operations")
Casual name: Department of State Authorization Act, Sec. 303 ("AI-enabled influence operations task force")
Short summary: Establishes a task force to counter AI-enabled disinformation, coordinate with academia and industry, and develop digital content provenance techniques. Requires reporting on progress and collaboration with international bodies to protect national security and mitigate AI misuse.
Long summary:
Establishes a Countering AI-Enabled Disinformation Task Force within 180 days to identify responses to AI-enabled disinformation threats.

Requires the Task Force to collaborate with private industry and academia to develop digital content provenance detection techniques.

Mandates internal coordination within the Department on AI-enabled disinformation issues.

Directs the Task Force to create a unified international approach for standards and frameworks to counter AI-enabled disinformation.

Requires the Task Force to identify necessary tools and resources for detecting AI-enabled disinformation, producing counter-messaging, expanding digital literacy, and coordinating with various stakeholders.

Mandates a report to Congress within one year on the Task Force’s establishment and progress.

Involves representatives from various relevant offices in the Task Force.

Defines key terms including "artificial intelligence" and "digital content provenance."

Official name: Department of State Authorization Act of 2023, Sec. 941 ("Reporting Related to the AUKUS Partnership")
Casual name: Department of State Authorization Act, Sec. 941 ("Reporting Related to AUKUS")
Short summary: Requires the President to report non-binding instruments related to the AUKUS partnership. Mandates biennial reports on AI and other tech collaboration under Pillar Two, assessing efforts in AI, quantum, cyber, and other advanced capabilities.
Long summary:
Mandates the President to submit the text of any non-binding instrument related to the AUKUS partnership to appropriate congressional committees within 30 days of finalization.

Excludes the need to resubmit texts already submitted under other legal provisions, while not relieving other legal submission requirements.

Defines "text" to include all related annexes, appendices, side agreements, and implementing documents.

Requires the Secretary, in coordination with other officials, to submit a biennial report on the AUKUS partnership starting one year after enactment.

Specifies that reports must cover defensive capability gaps, costs, industrial base contributions, military benefits, and assessments of Australia’s nuclear submarine contributions.

Demands updates on progress for Australia’s nuclear submarine development, infrastructure, workforce training, and support facilities.

Requires assessments of trilateral collaboration efforts in areas including underseas capabilities, quantum technologies, AI and autonomy, advanced cyber capabilities, hypersonic technologies, electronic warfare, innovation, and information sharing.

Official name: Atomic Energy Advancement Act, Sec. 123 ("Advancement of Nuclear Regulatory Oversight")
Casual name: Atomic Energy Advancement Act, Sec. 123 ("Nuclear Regulatory Oversight")
Short summary: Advances nuclear regulatory oversight by encouraging the use of AI and machine learning to enhance risk-informed oversight and inspection decisions. Promotes efficiency through improved technology integration and staff training, while assessing and enabling licensee adoption of advanced technologies.
Long summary:
Requires the Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) to report to Congress on actions taken during the COVID-19 emergency, including revised or suspended regulatory policies and their impacts.

Mandates NRC to develop a report within one year identifying improvements in nuclear oversight and inspections using risk-informed, performance-based procedures, information technologies, and AI.

Obligates NRC to seek input from stakeholders like the Secretary of Energy, National Laboratories, and the nuclear energy industry.

Directs NRC to assess the use of AI and machine learning to enhance oversight and inspection decisions.

Instructs NRC to identify specific actions for incorporating advanced technologies into their activities without compromising their mission.

Official name: Intelligence Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2024, Sec. 758 ("Implementation of Technology for Classification and Declassification")
Casual name: Intelligence Authorization Act FY 2024, Sec. 758 ("Technology for Classification and Declassification")
Short summary: Requires the Administrator to research AI and machine learning solutions for federal classification and declassification. Mandates submission of a technology recommendation to the President. Obligates the President to report to Congress on the recommended solution and adoption decision.
Long summary:
Requires the Administrator of the Office of Electronic Government to research a technology-based solution using machine learning and artificial intelligence for efficient classification and declassification systems within 1 year.

Mandates consultation with various key agencies, including the Secretary of Defense and CIA Director, to ensure a comprehensive approach.

Calls for the solution to be interoperable and federated across the Federal Government.

Obligates the Administrator to submit a recommendation to the President regarding the adoption of the researched technology-based solution.

Permits the Administrator to hire sufficient staff to execute the tasks outlined.

Directs the President to submit a classified report to Congress within 540 days, detailing the recommended solution and the decision on its adoption.

Official name: Health Care Price Transparency Act of 2023, Sec. 301 ("Establishing Requirements With Respect to the Use of Prior Authorization Under Medicare Advantage Plans")
Casual name: Health Care Price Transparency Act, Sec. 301 ("Requirements Regarding Prior Authorization Under Medicare Advantage Plans")
Short summary: Establishes electronic prior authorization programs for Medicare Advantage plans, requiring real-time decisions and transparency. Specifies enrollee protection standards, timely response requirements, and detailed reporting, including AI use. Mandates reports to Congress evaluating implementation and challenges.
Long summary:
Requires Medicare Advantage plans to establish an electronic prior authorization program by the third plan year after the enactment date.

Mandates that plans meet enrollee protection standards and transparency requirements by the fourth plan year.

Establishes a secure electronic transmission system for prior authorization requests and responses, excluding certain forms like facsimiles and non-compliant payer portals.

Specifies that real-time decisions must be made for certain prior authorization requests, with exceptions for extenuating circumstances.

Demands detailed annual reporting of prior authorization data, including the use of AI and decision-support technologies, and the publication of this information by the Secretary.

Calls for GAO and HHS to submit reports to Congress evaluating the implementation and impact of these requirements.

Official name: Rail Worker and Community Safety Act, Sec. 13 (Equipment Inspections)
Casual name: Rail Worker and Community Safety Act, Sec. 13 (Equipment Inspections)
Short summary: Require AI-based inspection technologies for rail inspections to be verified by a Qualified Mechanical Inspector.
Long summary:
Requires the Secretary of Transportation to issue regulations within 12 months of enactment.

Prohibits the movement of trains from switchyards before completing all required brake inspections.

Allows only Qualified Mechanical Inspectors to sign off on inspection forms for locomotives and rail cars.

Ensures that inspections using AI or camera-based systems under part 215 of title 49, Code of Federal Regulations, require verification by a Qualified Mechanical Inspector.

Official name: AI Grand Challenges Act of 2024
Casual name: AI Grand Challenges Act of 2024
Short summary: Establishes the AI Grand Challenges Program to award competitive prizes for AI research in sectors like health and cybersecurity, requiring public input, setting eligibility criteria, and mandating reports on winning submissions.
Long summary:
Establishes the AI Grand Challenges Program to incentivize AI research, development, and commercialization through prize competitions addressing grand challenges in various sectors like national security, health, and environment.

Mandates the Director of the National Science Foundation to coordinate with federal agencies and stakeholders, and public input, to identify and select AI grand challenges.

Designates a specific grand challenge for AI-enabled cancer breakthroughs.

Sets eligibility criteria for participants, requiring private entities to be U.S.-based and individuals to be U.S. citizens or permanent residents.

Requires regular reporting to Congress on the program’s progress and results, ensuring public accessibility of reports and competition information.
